revision.comment: Text replacement - "== See Also! ==" to "== See Also =="
revision.text:
{{Refimprove|date=October 2019}} {{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Kōtokui family | crest = Unknown.jpg | crest_caption = | main_house = {{ill|Kamo no Asomi clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} (branch family) | founder = {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoyuki|ja|幸徳井友幸}} | type = {{ill|Jige-ke|ja|地下家}}
{{ill|Shizoku|ja|士族}} | origin = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }} Kōtokui in Takahata Village | residence = Kōtokui, Takahata Village, Yamato Province
Present-day {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}} Takahatachō and Onyōchō | notable_members = {{ill|Kōtokui Tomokage|ja|幸徳井友景}} }} '''Kōtokui family''' (幸徳井家) refers to a {{ill|Jige-ke|ja|地下家}} based in {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良}}, engaged in {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}} as their hereditary occupation. Though originally descendants of the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏}}, the family became associated with the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} after {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoyuki|ja|幸徳井友幸}}, the founder, was adopted by the Kamo clan. {{see also|Kamo clan|Kadenokōji family (Kamo clan)}} == Overview == In 1391, Tomoyuki was born as the second son of {{ill|Abe no Tomouji|ja|安倍友氏}}, a branch family of the Abe clan. Tomoyuki studied {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}} under {{ill|Kamo no Sadahiro|ja|賀茂定弘}}, who served as {{ill|Gyōbu-kyō|ja|刑部卿}} and {{ill|Onmyōjō|ja|陰陽助}}. In 1419, Tomoyuki was adopted by Sadahiro and took the name Kamo no Tomoyuki. He rose to the rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}} by 1463 and established his residence in Kōtokui, a well-known area near {{ill|Takahata Village|ja|高畠村}}, serving as a political advisor to {{ill|Jinson|ja|尋尊}} of {{ill|Daijōin Temple|ja|大乗院}}. The family name "Kōtokui" derives from this location. After Tomoyuki's death in 1473, his descendants continued to serve Daijōin Temple and played key roles in supervising {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}} and {{ill|Shōmonshi|ja|声聞師}} in the Nara region, even compiling their own calendar, the {{ill|Kōtokui Calendar|ja|幸徳井暦}}. === Edo Period === In the early Edo period, {{ill|Tsuchimikado Yasuhiro|ja|土御門泰重}} resigned from the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} due to repeated failures, leaving a vacancy for {{ill|Onmyō no Kami|ja|陰陽頭}}. With the main Kamo family line extinct, {{ill|Kōtokui Tomokage|ja|幸徳井友景}}, the ninth head of the Kōtokui family, was appointed Onmyō no Kami in 1618. This appointment led to a series of Kōtokui family members, such as {{ill|Kōtokui Tomotane|ja|幸徳井友種}} and {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoden|ja|幸徳井友傳}}, succeeding to this position. However, by the late 17th century, the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}} regained the title of Onmyō no Kami and stripped the Kōtokui family of their control over southern Nara's Onmyōji. Despite this, the Kōtokui family maintained influence over Nara temples and continued advising on temple rituals. === Meiji Period and Beyond === Following the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the Kōtokui family petitioned to be ennobled as {{ill|Kazoku|ja|華族}} due to their historical contributions. The petition was denied, and their activities after this period remain unknown. == Heads of the Kōtokui Family == 1. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoyuki|ja|幸徳井友幸}} (1391–1473) - Senior Third Rank 2. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoshige|ja|幸徳井友重}} (1411–1490) - Senior Third Rank 3. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomonobu|ja|幸徳井友延}} (1435–1494) - Junior Third Rank ... 24. {{ill|Kōtokui Yasuhiko|ja|幸徳井保章}} (1847–?) == References in Literature == * {{Cite book|author=Arayama Toru|title=Yagyū Onmyōken|year=2008|isbn=978-4-10-121042-1}} * {{ill|Azano Kōhei|ja|あざの耕平}}, ''Tokyo Ravens'' - A {{ill|Light novel|ja|ライトノベル}} series featuring Onmyōji, including Kōtokui family members in a supporting role. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Chintaku Reifu Shrine|ja|鎮宅霊符神社}} * {{ill|Nara Educational University|ja|奈良教育大学}} * {{ill|Asuka Shrine (Nara)|ja|飛鳥神社}} [[ja:幸徳井家]] [[Category:Kōtokui Family]][[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kōtokui Family]][[Category:qq]] {{Refimprove|date=October 2019}} {{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Kōtokui family | crest = Unknown.jpg | crest_caption = | main_house = {{ill|Kamo no Asomi clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} (branch family) | founder = {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoyuki|ja|幸徳井友幸}} | type = {{ill|Jige-ke|ja|地下家}}
{{ill|Shizoku|ja|士族}} | origin = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }} Kōtokui in Takahata Village | residence = Kōtokui, Takahata Village, Yamato Province
Present-day {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}} Takahatachō and Onyōchō | notable_members = {{ill|Kōtokui Tomokage|ja|幸徳井友景}} }} '''Kōtokui family''' (幸徳井家) refers to a {{ill|Jige-ke|ja|地下家}} based in {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良}}, engaged in {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}} as their hereditary occupation. Though originally descendants of the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏}}, the family became associated with the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} after {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoyuki|ja|幸徳井友幸}}, the founder, was adopted by the Kamo clan. {{see also|Kamo clan|Kadenokōji family (Kamo clan)}} == Overview == In 1391, Tomoyuki was born as the second son of {{ill|Abe no Tomouji|ja|安倍友氏}}, a branch family of the Abe clan. Tomoyuki studied {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}} under {{ill|Kamo no Sadahiro|ja|賀茂定弘}}, who served as {{ill|Gyōbu-kyō|ja|刑部卿}} and {{ill|Onmyōjō|ja|陰陽助}}. In 1419, Tomoyuki was adopted by Sadahiro and took the name Kamo no Tomoyuki. He rose to the rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}} by 1463 and established his residence in Kōtokui, a well-known area near {{ill|Takahata Village|ja|高畠村}}, serving as a political advisor to {{ill|Jinson|ja|尋尊}} of {{ill|Daijōin Temple|ja|大乗院}}. The family name "Kōtokui" derives from this location. After Tomoyuki's death in 1473, his descendants continued to serve Daijōin Temple and played key roles in supervising {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}} and {{ill|Shōmonshi|ja|声聞師}} in the Nara region, even compiling their own calendar, the {{ill|Kōtokui Calendar|ja|幸徳井暦}}. === Edo Period === In the early Edo period, {{ill|Tsuchimikado Yasuhiro|ja|土御門泰重}} resigned from the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} due to repeated failures, leaving a vacancy for {{ill|Onmyō no Kami|ja|陰陽頭}}. With the main Kamo family line extinct, {{ill|Kōtokui Tomokage|ja|幸徳井友景}}, the ninth head of the Kōtokui family, was appointed Onmyō no Kami in 1618. This appointment led to a series of Kōtokui family members, such as {{ill|Kōtokui Tomotane|ja|幸徳井友種}} and {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoden|ja|幸徳井友傳}}, succeeding to this position. However, by the late 17th century, the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}} regained the title of Onmyō no Kami and stripped the Kōtokui family of their control over southern Nara's Onmyōji. Despite this, the Kōtokui family maintained influence over Nara temples and continued advising on temple rituals. === Meiji Period and Beyond === Following the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the Kōtokui family petitioned to be ennobled as {{ill|Kazoku|ja|華族}} due to their historical contributions. The petition was denied, and their activities after this period remain unknown. == Heads of the Kōtokui Family == 1. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoyuki|ja|幸徳井友幸}} (1391–1473) - Senior Third Rank 2. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoshige|ja|幸徳井友重}} (1411–1490) - Senior Third Rank 3. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomonobu|ja|幸徳井友延}} (1435–1494) - Junior Third Rank ... 24. {{ill|Kōtokui Yasuhiko|ja|幸徳井保章}} (1847–?) == References in Literature == * {{Cite book|author=Arayama Toru|title=Yagyū Onmyōken|year=2008|isbn=978-4-10-121042-1}} * {{ill|Azano Kōhei|ja|あざの耕平}}, ''Tokyo Ravens'' - A {{ill|Light novel|ja|ライトノベル}} series featuring Onmyōji, including Kōtokui family members in a supporting role. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Chintaku Reifu Shrine|ja|鎮宅霊符神社}} * {{ill|Nara Educational University|ja|奈良教育大学}} * {{Refimprove|date=October 2019}} {{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Kōtokui family | crest = Unknown.jpg | crest_caption = | main_house = {{ill|Kamo no Asomi clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} (branch family) | founder = {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoyuki|ja|幸徳井友幸}} | type = {{ill|Jige-ke|ja|地下家}}
{{ill|Shizoku|ja|士族}} | origin = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }} Kōtokui in Takahata Village | residence = Kōtokui, Takahata Village, Yamato Province
Present-day {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}} Takahatachō and Onyōchō | notable_members = {{ill|Kōtokui Tomokage|ja|幸徳井友景}} }} '''Kōtokui family''' (幸徳井家) refers to a {{ill|Jige-ke|ja|地下家}} based in {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良}}, engaged in {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}} as their hereditary occupation. Though originally descendants of the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏}}, the family became associated with the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} after {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoyuki|ja|幸徳井友幸}}, the founder, was adopted by the Kamo clan. {{see also|Kamo clan|Kadenokōji family (Kamo clan)}} == Overview == In 1391, Tomoyuki was born as the second son of {{ill|Abe no Tomouji|ja|安倍友氏}}, a branch family of the Abe clan. Tomoyuki studied {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}} under {{ill|Kamo no Sadahiro|ja|賀茂定弘}}, who served as {{ill|Gyōbu-kyō|ja|刑部卿}} and {{ill|Onmyōjō|ja|陰陽助}}. In 1419, Tomoyuki was adopted by Sadahiro and took the name Kamo no Tomoyuki. He rose to the rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}} by 1463 and established his residence in Kōtokui, a well-known area near {{ill|Takahata Village|ja|高畠村}}, serving as a political advisor to {{ill|Jinson|ja|尋尊}} of {{ill|Daijōin Temple|ja|大乗院}}. The family name "Kōtokui" derives from this location. After Tomoyuki's death in 1473, his descendants continued to serve Daijōin Temple and played key roles in supervising {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}} and {{ill|Shōmonshi|ja|声聞師}} in the Nara region, even compiling their own calendar, the {{ill|Kōtokui Calendar|ja|幸徳井暦}}. === Edo Period === In the early Edo period, {{ill|Tsuchimikado Yasuhiro|ja|土御門泰重}} resigned from the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} due to repeated failures, leaving a vacancy for {{ill|Onmyō no Kami|ja|陰陽頭}}. With the main Kamo family line extinct, {{ill|Kōtokui Tomokage|ja|幸徳井友景}}, the ninth head of the Kōtokui family, was appointed Onmyō no Kami in 1618. This appointment led to a series of Kōtokui family members, such as {{ill|Kōtokui Tomotane|ja|幸徳井友種}} and {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoden|ja|幸徳井友傳}}, succeeding to this position. However, by the late 17th century, the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}} regained the title of Onmyō no Kami and stripped the Kōtokui family of their control over southern Nara's Onmyōji. Despite this, the Kōtokui family maintained influence over Nara temples and continued advising on temple rituals. === Meiji Period and Beyond === Following the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the Kōtokui family petitioned to be ennobled as {{ill|Kazoku|ja|華族}} due to their historical contributions. The petition was denied, and their activities after this period remain unknown. == Heads of the Kōtokui Family == 1. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoyuki|ja|幸徳井友幸}} (1391–1473) - Senior Third Rank 2. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoshige|ja|幸徳井友重}} (1411–1490) - Senior Third Rank 3. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomonobu|ja|幸徳井友延}} (1435–1494) - Junior Third Rank ... 24. {{ill|Kōtokui Yasuhiko|ja|幸徳井保章}} (1847–?) == References in Literature revision.text:
{{Refimprove|date=October 2019}} {{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Kōtokui family | crest = Unknown.jpg | crest_caption = | main_house = {{ill|Kamo no Asomi clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} (branch family) | founder = {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoyuki|ja|幸徳井友幸}} | type = {{ill|Jige-ke|ja|地下家}}
{{ill|Shizoku|ja|士族}} | origin = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }} Kōtokui in Takahata Village | residence = Kōtokui, Takahata Village, Yamato Province
Present-day {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}} Takahatachō and Onyōchō | notable_members = {{ill|Kōtokui Tomokage|ja|幸徳井友景}} }} '''Kōtokui family''' (幸徳井家) refers to a {{ill|Jige-ke|ja|地下家}} based in {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良}}, engaged in {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}} as their hereditary occupation. Though originally descendants of the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏}}, the family became associated with the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} after {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoyuki|ja|幸徳井友幸}}, the founder, was adopted by the Kamo clan. {{see also|Kamo clan|Kadenokōji family (Kamo clan)}} == Overview == In 1391, Tomoyuki was born as the second son of {{ill|Abe no Tomouji|ja|安倍友氏}}, a branch family of the Abe clan. Tomoyuki studied {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}} under {{ill|Kamo no Sadahiro|ja|賀茂定弘}}, who served as {{ill|Gyōbu-kyō|ja|刑部卿}} and {{ill|Onmyōjō|ja|陰陽助}}. In 1419, Tomoyuki was adopted by Sadahiro and took the name Kamo no Tomoyuki. He rose to the rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}} by 1463 and established his residence in Kōtokui, a well-known area near {{ill|Takahata Village|ja|高畠村}}, serving as a political advisor to {{ill|Jinson|ja|尋尊}} of {{ill|Daijōin Temple|ja|大乗院}}. The family name "Kōtokui" derives from this location. After Tomoyuki's death in 1473, his descendants continued to serve Daijōin Temple and played key roles in supervising {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}} and {{ill|Shōmonshi|ja|声聞師}} in the Nara region, even compiling their own calendar, the {{ill|Kōtokui Calendar|ja|幸徳井暦}}. === Edo Period === In the early Edo period, {{ill|Tsuchimikado Yasuhiro|ja|土御門泰重}} resigned from the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} due to repeated failures, leaving a vacancy for {{ill|Onmyō no Kami|ja|陰陽頭}}. With the main Kamo family line extinct, {{ill|Kōtokui Tomokage|ja|幸徳井友景}}, the ninth head of the Kōtokui family, was appointed Onmyō no Kami in 1618. This appointment led to a series of Kōtokui family members, such as {{ill|Kōtokui Tomotane|ja|幸徳井友種}} and {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoden|ja|幸徳井友傳}}, succeeding to this position. However, by the late 17th century, the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}} regained the title of Onmyō no Kami and stripped the Kōtokui family of their control over southern Nara's Onmyōji. Despite this, the Kōtokui family maintained influence over Nara temples and continued advising on temple rituals. === Meiji Period and Beyond === Following the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the Kōtokui family petitioned to be ennobled as {{ill|Kazoku|ja|華族}} due to their historical contributions. The petition was denied, and their activities after this period remain unknown. == Heads of the Kōtokui Family == 1. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoyuki|ja|幸徳井友幸}} (1391–1473) - Senior Third Rank 2. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoshige|ja|幸徳井友重}} (1411–1490) - Senior Third Rank 3. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomonobu|ja|幸徳井友延}} (1435–1494) - Junior Third Rank ... 24. {{ill|Kōtokui Yasuhiko|ja|幸徳井保章}} (1847–?) == References in Literature == * {{Cite book|author=Arayama Toru|title=Yagyū Onmyōken|year=2008|isbn=978-4-10-121042-1}} * {{ill|Azano Kōhei|ja|あざの耕平}}, ''Tokyo Ravens'' - A {{ill|Light novel|ja|ライトノベル}} series featuring Onmyōji, including Kōtokui family members in a supporting role. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Chintaku Reifu Shrine|ja|鎮宅霊符神社}} * {{ill|Nara Educational University|ja|奈良教育大学}} * {{Refimprove|date=October 2019}} {{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Kōtokui family | crest = Unknown.jpg | crest_caption = | main_house = {{ill|Kamo no Asomi clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} (branch family) | founder = {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoyuki|ja|幸徳井友幸}} | type = {{ill|Jige-ke|ja|地下家}}
{{ill|Shizoku|ja|士族}} | origin = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }} Kōtokui in Takahata Village | residence = Kōtokui, Takahata Village, Yamato Province
Present-day {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}} Takahatachō and Onyōchō | notable_members = {{ill|Kōtokui Tomokage|ja|幸徳井友景}} }} '''Kōtokui family''' (幸徳井家) refers to a {{ill|Jige-ke|ja|地下家}} based in {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良}}, engaged in {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}} as their hereditary occupation. Though originally descendants of the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏}}, the family became associated with the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} after {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoyuki|ja|幸徳井友幸}}, the founder, was adopted by the Kamo clan. {{see also|Kamo clan|Kadenokōji family (Kamo clan)}} == Overview == In 1391, Tomoyuki was born as the second son of {{ill|Abe no Tomouji|ja|安倍友氏}}, a branch family of the Abe clan. Tomoyuki studied {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}} under {{ill|Kamo no Sadahiro|ja|賀茂定弘}}, who served as {{ill|Gyōbu-kyō|ja|刑部卿}} and {{ill|Onmyōjō|ja|陰陽助}}. In 1419, Tomoyuki was adopted by Sadahiro and took the name Kamo no Tomoyuki. He rose to the rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}} by 1463 and established his residence in Kōtokui, a well-known area near {{ill|Takahata Village|ja|高畠村}}, serving as a political advisor to {{ill|Jinson|ja|尋尊}} of {{ill|Daijōin Temple|ja|大乗院}}. The family name "Kōtokui" derives from this location. After Tomoyuki's death in 1473, his descendants continued to serve Daijōin Temple and played key roles in supervising {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}} and {{ill|Shōmonshi|ja|声聞師}} in the Nara region, even compiling their own calendar, the {{ill|Kōtokui Calendar|ja|幸徳井暦}}. === Edo Period === In the early Edo period, {{ill|Tsuchimikado Yasuhiro|ja|土御門泰重}} resigned from the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} due to repeated failures, leaving a vacancy for {{ill|Onmyō no Kami|ja|陰陽頭}}. With the main Kamo family line extinct, {{ill|Kōtokui Tomokage|ja|幸徳井友景}}, the ninth head of the Kōtokui family, was appointed Onmyō no Kami in 1618. This appointment led to a series of Kōtokui family members, such as {{ill|Kōtokui Tomotane|ja|幸徳井友種}} and {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoden|ja|幸徳井友傳}}, succeeding to this position. However, by the late 17th century, the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}} regained the title of Onmyō no Kami and stripped the Kōtokui family of their control over southern Nara's Onmyōji. Despite this, the Kōtokui family maintained influence over Nara temples and continued advising on temple rituals. === Meiji Period and Beyond === Following the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the Kōtokui family petitioned to be ennobled as {{ill|Kazoku|ja|華族}} due to their historical contributions. The petition was denied, and their activities after this period remain unknown. == Heads of the Kōtokui Family == 1. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoyuki|ja|幸徳井友幸}} (1391–1473) - Senior Third Rank 2. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoshige|ja|幸徳井友重}} (1411–1490) - Senior Third Rank 3. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomonobu|ja|幸徳井友延}} (1435–1494) - Junior Third Rank ... 24. {{ill|Kōtokui Yasuhiko|ja|幸徳井保章}} (1847–?) == References in Literature revision.comment: Bot: Reformatted page - moved categories, interwikis, DEFAULTSORT, and removed duplicates.
revision.text:
{{Refimprove|date=October 2019}} {{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Kōtokui family | crest = Unknown.jpg | crest_caption = | main_house = {{ill|Kamo no Asomi clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} (branch family) | founder = {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoyuki|ja|幸徳井友幸}} | type = {{ill|Jige-ke|ja|地下家}}
{{ill|Shizoku|ja|士族}} | origin = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }} Kōtokui in Takahata Village | residence = Kōtokui, Takahata Village, Yamato Province
Present-day {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}} Takahatachō and Onyōchō | notable_members = {{ill|Kōtokui Tomokage|ja|幸徳井友景}} }} '''Kōtokui family''' (幸徳井家) refers to a {{ill|Jige-ke|ja|地下家}} based in {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良}}, engaged in {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}} as their hereditary occupation. Though originally descendants of the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏}}, the family became associated with the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} after {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoyuki|ja|幸徳井友幸}}, the founder, was adopted by the Kamo clan. {{see also|Kamo clan|Kadenokōji family (Kamo clan)}} == Overview == In 1391, Tomoyuki was born as the second son of {{ill|Abe no Tomouji|ja|安倍友氏}}, a branch family of the Abe clan. Tomoyuki studied {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}} under {{ill|Kamo no Sadahiro|ja|賀茂定弘}}, who served as {{ill|Gyōbu-kyō|ja|刑部卿}} and {{ill|Onmyōjō|ja|陰陽助}}. In 1419, Tomoyuki was adopted by Sadahiro and took the name Kamo no Tomoyuki. He rose to the rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}} by 1463 and established his residence in Kōtokui, a well-known area near {{ill|Takahata Village|ja|高畠村}}, serving as a political advisor to {{ill|Jinson|ja|尋尊}} of {{ill|Daijōin Temple|ja|大乗院}}. The family name "Kōtokui" derives from this location. After Tomoyuki's death in 1473, his descendants continued to serve Daijōin Temple and played key roles in supervising {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}} and {{ill|Shōmonshi|ja|声聞師}} in the Nara region, even compiling their own calendar, the {{ill|Kōtokui Calendar|ja|幸徳井暦}}. === Edo Period === In the early Edo period, {{ill|Tsuchimikado Yasuhiro|ja|土御門泰重}} resigned from the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} due to repeated failures, leaving a vacancy for {{ill|Onmyō no Kami|ja|陰陽頭}}. With the main Kamo family line extinct, {{ill|Kōtokui Tomokage|ja|幸徳井友景}}, the ninth head of the Kōtokui family, was appointed Onmyō no Kami in 1618. This appointment led to a series of Kōtokui family members, such as {{ill|Kōtokui Tomotane|ja|幸徳井友種}} and {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoden|ja|幸徳井友傳}}, succeeding to this position. However, by the late 17th century, the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}} regained the title of Onmyō no Kami and stripped the Kōtokui family of their control over southern Nara's Onmyōji. Despite this, the Kōtokui family maintained influence over Nara temples and continued advising on temple rituals. === Meiji Period and Beyond === Following the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the Kōtokui family petitioned to be ennobled as {{ill|Kazoku|ja|華族}} due to their historical contributions. The petition was denied, and their activities after this period remain unknown. == Heads of the Kōtokui Family == 1. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoyuki|ja|幸徳井友幸}} (1391–1473) - Senior Third Rank 2. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoshige|ja|幸徳井友重}} (1411–1490) - Senior Third Rank 3. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomonobu|ja|幸徳井友延}} (1435–1494) - Junior Third Rank ... 24. {{ill|Kōtokui Yasuhiko|ja|幸徳井保章}} (1847–?) == References in Literature == * {{Cite book|author=Arayama Toru|title=Yagyū Onmyōken|year=2008|isbn=978-4-10-121042-1}} * {{ill|Azano Kōhei|ja|あざの耕平}}, ''Tokyo Ravens'' - A {{ill|Light novel|ja|ライトノベル}} series featuring Onmyōji, including Kōtokui family members in a supporting role. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Chintaku Reifu Shrine|ja|鎮宅霊符神社}} * {{ill|Nara Educational University|ja|奈良教育大学}} * {{ill|Asuka Shrine (Nara)|ja|飛鳥神社}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kōtokui Family]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kōtokui family|!]] [[Category:Kamo no Asomi clan|@]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Kinki region clans]] [[Category:Kuge]] [[Category:Onmyōryō]] [[Category:Onmyōdō]] [[Category:History of Nara]] [[Category:Sōjō District]] [[Category:Japanese-history-stub]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[ja:幸徳井家]] [[sn:Kōtokui Family]] [[en:Kōtokui Family]]

The petition was denied, and their activities after this period remain unknown. == Heads of the Kōtokui Family == 1. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoyuki|ja|幸徳井友幸|12=draft}} (1391–1473) - Senior Third Rank 2. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoshige|ja|幸徳井友重|12=draft}} (1411–1490) - Senior Third Rank 3. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomonobu|ja|幸徳井友延|12=draft}} (1435–1494) - Junior Third Rank ... 24. {{ill|Kōtokui Yasuhiko|ja|幸徳井保章|12=draft}} (1847–?) == References in Literature == * {{Cite book|author=Arayama Toru|title=Yagyū Onmyōken|year=2008|isbn=978-4-10-121042-1}} * {{ill|Azano Kōhei|ja|あざの耕平|12=draft}}, ''Tokyo Ravens'' - A {{ill|Light novel|ja|ライトノベル|12=draft}} series featuring Onmyōji, including Kōtokui family members in a supporting role. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Chintaku Reifu Shrine|ja|鎮宅霊符神社|12=draft}} * {{ill|Nara Educational University|ja|奈良教育大学|12=draft}} * {{ill|Asuka Shrine (Nara)|ja|飛鳥神社|12=draft}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kōtokui Family]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kōtokui family|!]] [[Category:Kamo no Asomi clan|@]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Kinki region clans]] [[Category:Kuge]] [[Category:Onmyōryō]] [[Category:Onmyōdō]] [[Category:History of Nara]] [[Category:Sōjō District]] [[Category:Japanese-history-stub]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[ja:幸徳井家]] [[sn:Kōtokui Family]] [[en:Kōtokui Family]]

revision.text:
{{Refimprove|date=October 2019}} {{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Kōtokui family | crest = Unknown.jpg | crest_caption = | main_house = {{ill|Kamo no Asomi clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=draft}} (branch family) | founder = {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoyuki|ja|幸徳井友幸|12=draft}} | type = {{ill|Jige-ke|ja|地下家|12=draft}}
{{ill|Shizoku|ja|士族|12=draft}} | origin = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|12=draft}} Kōtokui in Takahata Village | residence = Kōtokui, Takahata Village, Yamato Province
Present-day {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市|12=draft}} Takahatachō and Onyōchō | notable_members = {{ill|Kōtokui Tomokage|ja|幸徳井友景|12=draft}} }} '''Kōtokui family''' (幸徳井家) refers to a {{ill|Jige-ke|ja|地下家|12=draft}} based in {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良|12=draft}}, engaged in {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道|12=draft}} as their hereditary occupation. Though originally descendants of the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏|12=draft}}, the family became associated with the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=draft}} after {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoyuki|ja|幸徳井友幸|12=draft}}, the founder, was adopted by the Kamo clan. {{see also|Kamo clan|Kadenokōji family (Kamo clan)}} == Overview == In 1391, Tomoyuki was born as the second son of {{ill|Abe no Tomouji|ja|安倍友氏|12=draft}}, a branch family of the Abe clan. Tomoyuki studied {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道|12=draft}} under {{ill|Kamo no Sadahiro|ja|賀茂定弘|12=draft}}, who served as {{ill|Gyōbu-kyō|ja|刑部卿|12=draft}} and {{ill|Onmyōjō|ja|陰陽助|12=draft}}. In 1419, Tomoyuki was adopted by Sadahiro and took the name Kamo no Tomoyuki. He rose to the rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|12=draft}} by 1463 and established his residence in Kōtokui, a well-known area near {{ill|Takahata Village|ja|高畠村|12=draft}}, serving as a political advisor to {{ill|Jinson|ja|尋尊|12=draft}} of {{ill|Daijōin Temple|ja|大乗院|12=draft}}. The family name "Kōtokui" derives from this location. After Tomoyuki's death in 1473, his descendants continued to serve Daijōin Temple and played key roles in supervising {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師|12=draft}} and {{ill|Shōmonshi|ja|声聞師|12=draft}} in the Nara region, even compiling their own calendar, the {{ill|Kōtokui Calendar|ja|幸徳井暦|12=draft}}. === Edo Period === In the early Edo period, {{ill|Tsuchimikado Yasuhiro|ja|土御門泰重|12=draft}} resigned from the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|12=draft}} due to repeated failures, leaving a vacancy for {{ill|Onmyō no Kami|ja|陰陽頭|12=draft}}. With the main Kamo family line extinct, {{ill|Kōtokui Tomokage|ja|幸徳井友景|12=draft}}, the ninth head of the Kōtokui family, was appointed Onmyō no Kami in 1618. This appointment led to a series of Kōtokui family members, such as {{ill|Kōtokui Tomotane|ja|幸徳井友種|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoden|ja|幸徳井友傳|12=draft}}, succeeding to this position. However, by the late 17th century, the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=draft}} regained the title of Onmyō no Kami and stripped the Kōtokui family of their control over southern Nara's Onmyōji. Despite this, the Kōtokui family maintained influence over Nara temples and continued advising on temple rituals. === Meiji Period and Beyond === Following the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|12=draft}}, the Kōtokui family petitioned to be ennobled as {{ill|Kazoku|ja|華族|12=draft}} due to their historical contributions. The petition was denied, and their activities after this period remain unknown. == Heads of the Kōtokui Family == 1. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoyuki|ja|幸徳井友幸|12=draft}} (1391–1473) - Senior Third Rank 2. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoshige|ja|幸徳井友重|12=draft}} (1411–1490) - Senior Third Rank 3. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomonobu|ja|幸徳井友延|12=draft}} (1435–1494) - Junior Third Rank ... 24. {{ill|Kōtokui Yasuhiko|ja|幸徳井保章|12=draft}} (1847–?) == References in Literature == * {{Cite book|author=Arayama Toru|title=Yagyū Onmyōken|year=2008|isbn=978-4-10-121042-1}} * {{ill|Azano Kōhei|ja|あざの耕平|12=draft}}, ''Tokyo Ravens'' - A {{ill|Light novel|ja|ライトノベル|12=draft}} series featuring Onmyōji, including Kōtokui family members in a supporting role. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Chintaku Reifu Shrine|ja|鎮宅霊符神社|12=draft}} * {{ill|Nara Educational University|ja|奈良教育大学|12=draft}} * {{ill|Asuka Shrine (Nara)|ja|飛鳥神社|12=draft}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kōtokui Family]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kōtokui family|!]] [[Category:Kamo no Asomi clan|@]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Kinki region clans]] [[Category:Kuge]] [[Category:Onmyōryō]] [[Category:Onmyōdō]] [[Category:History of Nara]] [[Category:Sōjō District]] [[Category:Japanese-history-stub]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[ja:幸徳井家]] [[sn:Kōtokui Family]] [[en:Kōtokui Family]]

The petition was denied, and their activities after this period remain unknown. == Heads of the Kōtokui Family == 1. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoyuki|ja|幸徳井友幸|12=draft}} (1391–1473) - Senior Third Rank 2. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoshige|ja|幸徳井友重|12=draft}} (1411–1490) - Senior Third Rank 3. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomonobu|ja|幸徳井友延|12=draft}} (1435–1494) - Junior Third Rank ... 24. {{ill|Kōtokui Yasuhiko|ja|幸徳井保章|12=draft}} (1847–?) == References in Literature == * {{Cite book|author=Arayama Toru|title=Yagyū Onmyōken|year=2008|isbn=978-4-10-121042-1}} * {{ill|Azano Kōhei|ja|あざの耕平|12=draft}}, ''Tokyo Ravens'' - A {{ill|Light novel|ja|ライトノベル|12=draft}} series featuring Onmyōji, including Kōtokui family members in a supporting role. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Chintaku Reifu Shrine|ja|鎮宅霊符神社|12=draft}} * {{ill|Nara Educational University|ja|奈良教育大学|12=draft}} * {{ill|Asuka Shrine (Nara)|ja|飛鳥神社|12=draft}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kōtokui Family]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kōtokui family|!]] [[Category:Kamo no Asomi clan|@]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Kinki region clans]] [[Category:Kuge]] [[Category:Onmyōryō]] [[Category:Onmyōdō]] [[Category:History of Nara]] [[Category:Sōjō District]] [[Category:Japanese-history-stub]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:幸徳井家]] [[sn:Kōtokui Family]] [[en:Kōtokui Family]]

revision.text:
{{Refimprove|date=October 2019}} {{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Kōtokui family | crest = Unknown.jpg | crest_caption = | main_house = {{ill|Kamo no Asomi clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=draft}} (branch family) | founder = {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoyuki|ja|幸徳井友幸|12=draft}} | type = {{ill|Jige-ke|ja|地下家|12=draft}}
{{ill|Shizoku|ja|士族|12=draft}} | origin = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|12=draft}} Kōtokui in Takahata Village | residence = Kōtokui, Takahata Village, Yamato Province
Present-day {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市|12=draft}} Takahatachō and Onyōchō | notable_members = {{ill|Kōtokui Tomokage|ja|幸徳井友景|12=draft}} }} '''Kōtokui family''' (幸徳井家) refers to a {{ill|Jige-ke|ja|地下家|12=draft}} based in {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良|12=draft}}, engaged in {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道|12=draft}} as their hereditary occupation. Though originally descendants of the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏|12=draft}}, the family became associated with the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=draft}} after {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoyuki|ja|幸徳井友幸|12=draft}}, the founder, was adopted by the Kamo clan. {{see also|Kamo clan|Kadenokōji family (Kamo clan)}} == Overview == In 1391, Tomoyuki was born as the second son of {{ill|Abe no Tomouji|ja|安倍友氏|12=draft}}, a branch family of the Abe clan. Tomoyuki studied {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道|12=draft}} under {{ill|Kamo no Sadahiro|ja|賀茂定弘|12=draft}}, who served as {{ill|Gyōbu-kyō|ja|刑部卿|12=draft}} and {{ill|Onmyōjō|ja|陰陽助|12=draft}}. In 1419, Tomoyuki was adopted by Sadahiro and took the name Kamo no Tomoyuki. He rose to the rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|12=draft}} by 1463 and established his residence in Kōtokui, a well-known area near {{ill|Takahata Village|ja|高畠村|12=draft}}, serving as a political advisor to {{ill|Jinson|ja|尋尊|12=draft}} of {{ill|Daijōin Temple|ja|大乗院|12=draft}}. The family name "Kōtokui" derives from this location. After Tomoyuki's death in 1473, his descendants continued to serve Daijōin Temple and played key roles in supervising {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師|12=draft}} and {{ill|Shōmonshi|ja|声聞師|12=draft}} in the Nara region, even compiling their own calendar, the {{ill|Kōtokui Calendar|ja|幸徳井暦|12=draft}}. === Edo Period === In the early Edo period, {{ill|Tsuchimikado Yasuhiro|ja|土御門泰重|12=draft}} resigned from the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=draft}} due to repeated failures, leaving a vacancy for {{ill|Onmyō no Kami|ja|陰陽頭|12=draft}}. With the main Kamo family line extinct, {{ill|Kōtokui Tomokage|ja|幸徳井友景|12=draft}}, the ninth head of the Kōtokui family, was appointed Onmyō no Kami in 1618. This appointment led to a series of Kōtokui family members, such as {{ill|Kōtokui Tomotane|ja|幸徳井友種|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoden|ja|幸徳井友傳|12=draft}}, succeeding to this position. However, by the late 17th century, the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=draft}} regained the title of Onmyō no Kami and stripped the Kōtokui family of their control over southern Nara's Onmyōji. Despite this, the Kōtokui family maintained influence over Nara temples and continued advising on temple rituals. === Meiji Period and Beyond === Following the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|12=draft}}, the Kōtokui family petitioned to be ennobled as {{ill|Kazoku|ja|華族|12=draft}} due to their historical contributions. The petition was denied, and their activities after this period remain unknown. == Heads of the Kōtokui Family == 1. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoyuki|ja|幸徳井友幸|12=draft}} (1391–1473) - Senior Third Rank 2. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoshige|ja|幸徳井友重|12=draft}} (1411–1490) - Senior Third Rank 3. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomonobu|ja|幸徳井友延|12=draft}} (1435–1494) - Junior Third Rank ... 24. {{ill|Kōtokui Yasuhiko|ja|幸徳井保章|12=draft}} (1847–?) == References in Literature == * {{Cite book|author=Arayama Toru|title=Yagyū Onmyōken|year=2008|isbn=978-4-10-121042-1}} * {{ill|Azano Kōhei|ja|あざの耕平|12=draft}}, ''Tokyo Ravens'' - A {{ill|Light novel|ja|ライトノベル|12=draft}} series featuring Onmyōji, including Kōtokui family members in a supporting role. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Chintaku Reifu Shrine|ja|鎮宅霊符神社|12=draft}} * {{ill|Nara Educational University|ja|奈良教育大学|12=draft}} * {{ill|Asuka Shrine (Nara)|ja|飛鳥神社|12=draft}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kōtokui Family]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kōtokui family|!]] [[Category:Kamo no Asomi clan|@]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Kinki region clans]] [[Category:Kuge]] [[Category:Onmyōryō]] [[Category:Onmyōdō]] [[Category:History of Nara]] [[Category:Sōjō District]] [[Category:Japanese-history-stub]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:幸徳井家]] [[sn:Kōtokui Family]] [[en:Kōtokui Family]]

The petition was denied, and their activities after this period remain unknown. == Heads of the Kōtokui Family == 1. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoyuki|ja|幸徳井友幸|12=draft}} (1391–1473) - Senior Third Rank 2. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoshige|ja|幸徳井友重|12=draft}} (1411–1490) - Senior Third Rank 3. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomonobu|ja|幸徳井友延|12=draft}} (1435–1494) - Junior Third Rank ... 24. {{ill|Kōtokui Yasuhiko|ja|幸徳井保章|12=draft}} (1847–?) == References in Literature == * {{Cite book|author=Arayama Toru|title=Yagyū Onmyōken|year=2008|isbn=978-4-10-121042-1}} * {{ill|Azano Kōhei|ja|あざの耕平|12=draft}}, ''Tokyo Ravens'' - A {{ill|Light novel|ja|ライトノベル|12=draft}} series featuring Onmyōji, including Kōtokui family members in a supporting role. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Chintaku Reifu Shrine|ja|鎮宅霊符神社|12=draft}} * {{ill|Nara Educational University|ja|奈良教育大学|12=draft}} * {{ill|Asuka Shrine (Nara)|ja|飛鳥神社|12=draft}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kōtokui Family]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kōtokui family|!]] [[Category:Kamo no Asomi clan|@]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Kinki region clans]] [[Category:Kuge]] [[Category:Onmyōryō]] [[Category:Onmyōdō]] [[Category:History of Nara]] [[Category:Sōjō District]] [[Category:Japanese-history-stub]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:幸徳井家]] [[sn:Kōtokui Family]] [[en:Kōtokui Family]] [[Category:Ambox transclusions]]

{{Refimprove|date=October 2019}} {{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Kōtokui family | crest = Unknown.jpg | crest_caption = | main_house = {{ill|Kamo no Asomi clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=draft}} (branch family) | founder = {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoyuki|ja|幸徳井友幸|12=draft}} | type = {{ill|Jige-ke|ja|地下家|12=draft}}
{{ill|Shizoku|ja|士族|12=draft}} | origin = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|12=draft}} Kōtokui in Takahata Village | residence = Kōtokui, Takahata Village, Yamato Province
Present-day {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市|12=draft}} Takahatachō and Onyōchō | notable_members = {{ill|Kōtokui Tomokage|ja|幸徳井友景|12=draft}} }} '''Kōtokui family''' (幸徳井家) refers to a {{ill|Jige-ke|ja|地下家|12=draft}} based in {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良|12=draft}}, engaged in {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道|12=draft}} as their hereditary occupation. Though originally descendants of the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏|12=draft}}, the family became associated with the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=draft}} after {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoyuki|ja|幸徳井友幸|12=draft}}, the founder, was adopted by the Kamo clan. {{see also|Kamo clan|Kadenokōji family (Kamo clan)}} == Overview == In 1391, Tomoyuki was born as the second son of {{ill|Abe no Tomouji|ja|安倍友氏|12=draft}}, a branch family of the Abe clan. Tomoyuki studied {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道|12=draft}} under {{ill|Kamo no Sadahiro|ja|賀茂定弘|12=draft}}, who served as {{ill|Gyōbu-kyō|ja|刑部卿|12=draft}} and {{ill|Onmyōjō|ja|陰陽助|12=draft}}. In 1419, Tomoyuki was adopted by Sadahiro and took the name Kamo no Tomoyuki. He rose to the Rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|12=draft}} by 1463 and established his residence in Kōtokui, a well-known area near {{ill|Takahata Village|ja|高畠村|12=draft}}, serving as a political advisor to {{ill|Jinson|ja|尋尊|12=draft}} of {{ill|Daijōin Temple|ja|大乗院|12=draft}}. The family name "Kōtokui" derives from this location. After Tomoyuki's death in 1473, his descendants continued to serve Daijōin Temple and played key roles in supervising {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師|12=draft}} and {{ill|Shōmonshi|ja|声聞師|12=draft}} in the Nara region, even compiling their own calendar, the {{ill|Kōtokui Calendar|ja|幸徳井暦|12=draft}}. === Edo Period === In the early Edo period, {{ill|Tsuchimikado Yasuhiro|ja|土御門泰重|12=draft}} resigned from the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=draft}} due to repeated failures, leaving a vacancy for {{ill|Onmyō no Kami|ja|陰陽頭|12=draft}}. With the main Kamo family line extinct, {{ill|Kōtokui Tomokage|ja|幸徳井友景|12=draft}}, the ninth head of the Kōtokui family, was appointed Onmyō no Kami in 1618. This appointment led to a series of Kōtokui family members, such as {{ill|Kōtokui Tomotane|ja|幸徳井友種|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoden|ja|幸徳井友傳|12=draft}}, succeeding to this position. However, by the late 17th century, the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=draft}} regained the title of Onmyō no Kami and stripped the Kōtokui family of their control over southern Nara's Onmyōji. Despite this, the Kōtokui family maintained influence over Nara temples and continued advising on temple rituals. === Meiji Period and Beyond === Following the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|12=draft}}, the Kōtokui family petitioned to be ennobled as {{ill|Kazoku|ja|華族|12=draft}} due to their historical contributions. The petition was denied, and their activities after this period remain unknown. == Heads of the Kōtokui Family == 1. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoyuki|ja|幸徳井友幸|12=draft}} (1391–1473) - Senior Third Rank 2. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoshige|ja|幸徳井友重|12=draft}} (1411–1490) - Senior Third Rank 3. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomonobu|ja|幸徳井友延|12=draft}} (1435–1494) - Junior Third Rank ... 24. {{ill|Kōtokui Yasuhiko|ja|幸徳井保章|12=draft}} (1847–?) == References in Literature == * {{Cite book|author=Arayama Toru|title=Yagyū Onmyōken|year=2008|isbn=978-4-10-121042-1}} * {{ill|Azano Kōhei|ja|あざの耕平|12=draft}}, ''Tokyo Ravens'' - A {{ill|Light novel|ja|ライトノベル|12=draft}} series featuring Onmyōji, including Kōtokui family members in a supporting role. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Chintaku Reifu Shrine|ja|鎮宅霊符神社|12=draft}} * {{ill|Nara Educational University|ja|奈良教育大学|12=draft}} * {{ill|Asuka Shrine (Nara)|ja|飛鳥神社|12=draft}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kōtokui Family]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kōtokui family|!]] [[Category:Kamo no Asomi clan|@]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Kinki region clans]] [[Category:Kuge]] [[Category:Onmyōryō]] [[Category:Onmyōdō]] [[Category:History of Nara]] [[Category:Sōjō District]] [[Category:Japanese-history-stub]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:幸徳井家]] [[sn:Kōtokui Family]] [[en:Kōtokui Family]] [[Category:Ambox transclusions]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kōtokui Family]][[Category:qq]]

{{Refimprove|date=October 2019}} {{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Kōtokui family | crest = Unknown.jpg | crest_caption = | main_house = {{ill|Kamo no Asomi clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} (branch family) | founder = {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoyuki|ja|幸徳井友幸}} | type = {{ill|Jige-ke|ja|地下家}}

revision.comment: 38 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:幸徳井家 up to rev 100215448
revision.text:
{{脚注の不足|date=2019-10}} {{日本の氏族 |家名= 幸徳井家 |家紋= Unknown.jpg |家紋名称= |本姓= [[賀茂朝臣氏|賀茂]][[朝臣]][[庶家|庶流]] |家祖= [[幸徳井友幸]] |種別= [[地下家]]
[[士族]] |出身地= [[大和国]][[高畑町|高畠幸村]]幸徳井 |根拠地= 大和国高畠幸村幸徳井
[[奈良市]][[高畑町]]・[[陰陽町]] |人物= [[幸徳井友景]] |支流= }} '''幸徳井家'''（こうとくいけ / かでいけ）は、南都[[奈良]]を根拠地とした[[陰陽道]]を[[家業]]とする[[地下家]]の[[官人]]。[[阿倍氏#陰陽道安倍氏|安倍氏]]の[[親族|末裔]]であるが、初代[[幸徳井友幸]]が[[賀茂朝臣氏|賀茂氏]]の[[養子縁組|養子]]ととなったために、賀茂氏系とされるのが一般的である。 {{see also|賀茂朝臣氏|勘解由小路家 (賀茂氏)}} == 概要 == [[明徳]]2年（[[1391年]]）に安倍氏庶流の[[安倍友氏]]の次男として生まれた友徳は、初め[[刑部省|刑部卿]]兼[[陰陽寮|陰陽助]]の[[賀茂定弘]]より[[陰陽道]]を学んでいたが、[[応永]]26年（[[1419年]]）にその養子となり'''賀茂友幸'''と名乗った。友幸は応永30年（[[1423年]]）10月に[[従五位|従五位下]]に[[叙位|叙任]]され、[[享徳]]2年（[[1454年]]）に[[従三位]]、[[寛正]]4年（[[1463年]]）には[[正三位]]となるが、後に奈良郊外の[[高畠幸村]]（たかはたさいわいむら）の[[幸徳井]]{{efn2|[[幸徳井町]]、のちの[[幸井町]]、現在の[[紀寺町]]幸町。幸町バス停の[[住所|所在地]]で、[[志賀直哉旧居 (奈良市高畑)]]の元の所在地。[[高畑町 (奈良市)|高畑町]]の隣町。}}という井戸のある地に居宅を構えて[[興福寺]][[大乗院 (門跡寺院)|大乗院]][[門跡]][[尋尊]]に仕えた。友幸は尋尊の元で陰陽道や[[暦道]]のことのみならず、政治顧問的な役目を果たしていたとされ、その居住地より「幸徳井」の[[家名]]を号した。[[文明_(日本)|文明]]5年（[[1473年]]）に友幸が83歳で[[崩御#薨去|薨去]]した後も、子の[[幸徳井友重|友重]]も尋尊に仕えてその推挙で正三位まで昇り、次の[[幸徳井友延|友延]]も従三位に昇った。以後、幸徳井家は興福寺と事実上の主従関係を結び、その力を背景に南都社寺への日時勘申を初め、[[陰陽師]]・[[声聞師]]の監督など南都に関する陰陽道・[[暦道]]に関する全てを取り仕切り、政情不安で機能停止した[[京都]]の[[陰陽寮]]に代わって幸徳井家による独自の暦（[[幸徳井暦]]）が編纂されたこともあった。 [[江戸時代]]に入って当時の安倍氏[[家長|当主]]であった[[土御門泰重]]が度々の不手際による失意から、陰陽寮を辞することを表明するが、かつて安倍氏と並ぶ陰陽道の元締とされていた賀茂氏[[宗家]]であった[[勘解由小路家 (賀茂氏)|勘解由小路家]]は断絶していた{{Efn2|[[賀茂朝臣氏|賀茂姓]][[勘解由小路家 (賀茂氏)|勘解由小路家]]断絶の顚末については、[[賀茂在昌]]参照。}}ため、[[元和 (日本)|元和]]4年（[[1618年]]）に幸徳井家9代目にあたる[[幸徳井友景]]{{Efn2|[[柳生宗厳]]の[[甥]]であり、幸徳井家の養子となった。『[[#koutokui|幸徳井世系考訂本]]』によると、[[安井永順]]の子で柳生宗厳の養子とある。}}が同家では初の陰陽頭に任ぜられた[[#koutokui|幸徳井世系考訂本]]。以後、[[幸徳井友種|友種]]・[[幸徳井友傳|友傳]]と幸徳井家出身の陰陽頭が続くが、後に泰重がこれを後悔して幸徳井家より陰陽頭の地位を取り戻そうとした。だが、泰重が失った信頼が回復できずに[[土御門家#土御門家（安倍氏）|土御門家]]は不遇の時代を迎える。 だが、[[寛文]]10年（[[1670年]]）に[[土御門泰福]]が陰陽頭の地位のみならず、全国の陰陽師・声聞師の支配権を主張して陰陽頭だった友傳に相論を仕掛けた。友傳は興福寺の支援を受けてこれに抵抗するが、[[天和 (日本)|天和]]2年（[[1682年]]）に友傳が35歳で急死してしまう。後継者の[[幸徳井友信|友信]]は幼少であるために、土御門泰福が後任の陰陽頭に任じられ、翌年には諸国の陰陽師を支配・免許の権限が与えられて、幸徳井家が持っていた南都陰陽師の支配権も没収した。更に[[元禄]]2年（[[1699年]]）には友信に圧力をかけて、土御門家のみを陰陽道宗家として幸徳井家はその配下となることを誓約させられた。以後、幸徳井家は代々陰陽助として土御門家の指示のもとに[[暦注]]のみを管轄した（ただし、[[宝暦]]4年（[[1754年]]）に土御門家内部の混乱から[[幸徳井保暠]]が陰陽頭に[[任官|任命]]された例外がある）。ただし、土御門家といえども幸徳井家と興福寺などの南都社寺との関係に直接干渉することは出来ず、南都社寺への日時勘申などは引き続き幸徳井家が担当している。 [[明治維新]]後、歴代中[[公卿]]を6名輩出した実績を根拠に[[華族]]への取立てを請願するも、[[明治政府]]より却下されている。その後、[[現代 (時代区分)|現代]]に至るまで消息は不明。 == 歴代当主 == # [[幸徳井友幸]]（明徳2年（1391年） - 文明5年（1473年）） - [[正三位]] # [[幸徳井友重]]（応永18年（1411年） - 延徳2年（1490年）） - 正三位 # [[幸徳井友延]]（永享7年（1435年） - 明応3年（1494年）） - [[従三位]] # [[幸徳井友胤]]（寛正3年（1462年） - 文亀3年（1503年）） # [[幸徳井友栄]]（文明19年/長享元年（1487年） - 弘治4年/永禄元年（1558年）） # [[幸徳井友祐]]（永正4年（1507年） - 天文15年（1546年）） # [[幸徳井友忠]]（享禄4年（1531年） - 慶長6年（1601年）） - 従三位 # [[幸徳井友豊]]（永禄9年（1566年） - 慶長7年（1602年）） # [[幸徳井友景]]（天正11年（1583年） - 寛永21年/正保元年/正保2年（1645年）） - [[陰陽寮|陰陽]]頭　[[柳生宗厳]]の甥であり、幸徳井家の養子となった。 # [[幸徳井友種]]（慶長13年（1608年） - 明暦4年/万治元年（1658年）） - 陰陽頭 # [[幸徳井友傳]]（正保5年/慶安元年（1648年） - 天和2年（1682年）） - 陰陽頭 # [[幸徳井友信]]（寛文6年（1666年） - 享保8年（1723年）） # [[幸徳井友親]]（寛文11年（1671年） - 享保14年（1729年）） # [[幸徳井保篤]]（元禄15年（1702年） - 延享5年/寛延元年（1748年）） # [[幸徳井保屋]]（正徳6年/享保元年（1716年） - 寛保3年（1743年）） # [[幸徳井保暠]]（享保15年（1730年） - 享和2年（1802年）） - 従三位/陰陽頭 # [[幸徳井保敬]]（寛保4年/延享元年（1744年） - 文政2年（1819年）） - 従三位 # [[幸徳井保教]]（明和4年（1767年） - 寛政11年（1799年）） # [[幸徳井保雅]]（天明6年（1786年）/天明7年（1787年） - 文化4年（1807年）） # [[幸徳井保孝]]（享和3年（1803年） - 文政6年（1823年）） # [[幸徳井保救]]（安永6年（1777年） - 嘉永4年/嘉永5年（[[1852年]]）） # [[幸徳井保真]]（寛政8年（1796年） - 文化9年（1812年）） # [[幸徳井保源]]（文政2年（1819年） - 1872年（[[明治4年]]/[[明治5年]]） # [[幸徳井保章]]（弘化4年（1847年） - [[疑問符|?]]） * [[参考文献|出典]]：[[#koutokui|幸徳井世系考訂本]] == 登場作品 == * {{Cite revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[幸徳井家]] to [[Kōtokui Family]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{脚注の不足|date=2019-10}} {{日本の氏族 |家名= 幸徳井家 |家紋= Unknown.jpg |家紋名称= |本姓= [[賀茂朝臣氏|賀茂]][[朝臣]][[庶家|庶流]] |家祖= [[幸徳井友幸]] |種別= [[地下家]]
[[士族]] |出身地= [[大和国]][[高畑町|高畠幸村]]幸徳井 |根拠地= 大和国高畠幸村幸徳井
[[奈良市]][[高畑町]]・[[陰陽町]] |人物= [[幸徳井友景]] |支流= }} '''幸徳井家'''（こうとくいけ / かでいけ）は、南都[[奈良]]を根拠地とした[[陰陽道]]を[[家業]]とする[[地下家]]の[[官人]]。[[阿倍氏#陰陽道安倍氏|安倍氏]]の[[親族|末裔]]であるが、初代[[幸徳井友幸]]が[[賀茂朝臣氏|賀茂氏]]の[[養子縁組|養子]]ととなったために、賀茂氏系とされるのが一般的である。 {{see also|賀茂朝臣氏|勘解由小路家 (賀茂氏)}} == 概要 == [[明徳]]2年（[[1391年]]）に安倍氏庶流の[[安倍友氏]]の次男として生まれた友徳は、初め[[刑部省|刑部卿]]兼[[陰陽寮|陰陽助]]の[[賀茂定弘]]より[[陰陽道]]を学んでいたが、[[応永]]26年（[[1419年]]）にその養子となり'''賀茂友幸'''と名乗った。友幸は応永30年（[[1423年]]）10月に[[従五位|従五位下]]に[[叙位|叙任]]され、[[享徳]]2年（[[1454年]]）に[[従三位]]、[[寛正]]4年（[[1463年]]）には[[正三位]]となるが、後に奈良郊外の[[高畠幸村]]（たかはたさいわいむら）の[[幸徳井]]{{efn2|[[幸徳井町]]、のちの[[幸井町]]、現在の[[紀寺町]]幸町。幸町バス停の[[住所|所在地]]で、[[志賀直哉旧居 (奈良市高畑)]]の元の所在地。[[高畑町 (奈良市)|高畑町]]の隣町。}}という井戸のある地に居宅を構えて[[興福寺]][[大乗院 (門跡寺院)|大乗院]][[門跡]][[尋尊]]に仕えた。友幸は尋尊の元で陰陽道や[[暦道]]のことのみならず、政治顧問的な役目を果たしていたとされ、その居住地より「幸徳井」の[[家名]]を号した。[[文明_(日本)|文明]]5年（[[1473年]]）に友幸が83歳で[[崩御#薨去|薨去]]した後も、子の[[幸徳井友重|友重]]も尋尊に仕えてその推挙で正三位まで昇り、次の[[幸徳井友延|友延]]も従三位に昇った。以後、幸徳井家は興福寺と事実上の主従関係を結び、その力を背景に南都社寺への日時勘申を初め、[[陰陽師]]・[[声聞師]]の監督など南都に関する陰陽道・[[暦道]]に関する全てを取り仕切り、政情不安で機能停止した[[京都]]の[[陰陽寮]]に代わって幸徳井家による独自の暦（[[幸徳井暦]]）が編纂されたこともあった。 [[江戸時代]]に入って当時の安倍氏[[家長|当主]]であった[[土御門泰重]]が度々の不手際による失意から、陰陽寮を辞することを表明するが、かつて安倍氏と並ぶ陰陽道の元締とされていた賀茂氏[[宗家]]であった[[勘解由小路家 (賀茂氏)|勘解由小路家]]は断絶していた{{Efn2|[[賀茂朝臣氏|賀茂姓]][[勘解由小路家 (賀茂氏)|勘解由小路家]]断絶の顚末については、[[賀茂在昌]]参照。}}ため、[[元和 (日本)|元和]]4年（[[1618年]]）に幸徳井家9代目にあたる[[幸徳井友景]]{{Efn2|[[柳生宗厳]]の[[甥]]であり、幸徳井家の養子となった。『[[#koutokui|幸徳井世系考訂本]]』によると、[[安井永順]]の子で柳生宗厳の養子とある。}}が同家では初の陰陽頭に任ぜられた[[#koutokui|幸徳井世系考訂本]]。以後、[[幸徳井友種|友種]]・[[幸徳井友傳|友傳]]と幸徳井家出身の陰陽頭が続くが、後に泰重がこれを後悔して幸徳井家より陰陽頭の地位を取り戻そうとした。だが、泰重が失った信頼が回復できずに[[土御門家#土御門家（安倍氏）|土御門家]]は不遇の時代を迎える。 だが、[[寛文]]10年（[[1670年]]）に[[土御門泰福]]が陰陽頭の地位のみならず、全国の陰陽師・声聞師の支配権を主張して陰陽頭だった友傳に相論を仕掛けた。友傳は興福寺の支援を受けてこれに抵抗するが、[[天和 (日本)|天和]]2年（[[1682年]]）に友傳が35歳で急死してしまう。後継者の[[幸徳井友信|友信]]は幼少であるために、土御門泰福が後任の陰陽頭に任じられ、翌年には諸国の陰陽師を支配・免許の権限が与えられて、幸徳井家が持っていた南都陰陽師の支配権も没収した。更に[[元禄]]2年（[[1699年]]）には友信に圧力をかけて、土御門家のみを陰陽道宗家として幸徳井家はその配下となることを誓約させられた。以後、幸徳井家は代々陰陽助として土御門家の指示のもとに[[暦注]]のみを管轄した（ただし、[[宝暦]]4年（[[1754年]]）に土御門家内部の混乱から[[幸徳井保暠]]が陰陽頭に[[任官|任命]]された例外がある）。ただし、土御門家といえども幸徳井家と興福寺などの南都社寺との関係に直接干渉することは出来ず、南都社寺への日時勘申などは引き続き幸徳井家が担当している。 [[明治維新]]後、歴代中[[公卿]]を6名輩出した実績を根拠に[[華族]]への取立てを請願するも、[[明治政府]]より却下されている。その後、[[現代 (時代区分)|現代]]に至るまで消息は不明。 == 歴代当主 == # [[幸徳井友幸]]（明徳2年（1391年） - 文明5年（1473年）） - [[正三位]] # [[幸徳井友重]]（応永18年（1411年） - 延徳2年（1490年）） - 正三位 # [[幸徳井友延]]（永享7年（1435年） - 明応3年（1494年）） - [[従三位]] # [[幸徳井友胤]]（寛正3年（1462年） - 文亀3年（1503年）） # [[幸徳井友栄]]（文明19年/長享元年（1487年） - 弘治4年/永禄元年（1558年）） # [[幸徳井友祐]]（永正4年（1507年） - 天文15年（1546年）） # [[幸徳井友忠]]（享禄4年（1531年） - 慶長6年（1601年）） - 従三位 # [[幸徳井友豊]]（永禄9年（1566年） - 慶長7年（1602年）） # [[幸徳井友景]]（天正11年（1583年） - 寛永21年/正保元年/正保2年（1645年）） - [[陰陽寮|陰陽]]頭　[[柳生宗厳]]の甥であり、幸徳井家の養子となった。 # [[幸徳井友種]]（慶長13年（1608年） - 明暦4年/万治元年（1658年）） - 陰陽頭 # [[幸徳井友傳]]（正保5年/慶安元年（1648年） - 天和2年（1682年）） - 陰陽頭 # [[幸徳井友信]]（寛文6年（1666年） - 享保8年（1723年）） # [[幸徳井友親]]（寛文11年（1671年） - 享保14年（1729年）） # [[幸徳井保篤]]（元禄15年（1702年） - 延享5年/寛延元年（1748年）） # [[幸徳井保屋]]（正徳6年/享保元年（1716年） - 寛保3年（1743年）） # [[幸徳井保暠]]（享保15年（1730年） - 享和2年（1802年）） - 従三位/陰陽頭 # [[幸徳井保敬]]（寛保4年/延享元年（1744年） - 文政2年（1819年）） - 従三位 # [[幸徳井保教]]（明和4年（1767年） - 寛政11年（1799年）） # [[幸徳井保雅]]（天明6年（1786年）/天明7年（1787年） - 文化4年（1807年）） # [[幸徳井保孝]]（享和3年（1803年） - 文政6年（1823年）） # [[幸徳井保救]]（安永6年（1777年） - 嘉永4年/嘉永5年（[[1852年]]）） # [[幸徳井保真]]（寛政8年（1796年） - 文化9年（1812年）） # [[幸徳井保源]]（文政2年（1819年） - 1872年（[[明治4年]]/[[明治5年]]） # [[幸徳井保章]]（弘化4年（1847年） - [[疑問符|?]]） * [[参考文献|出典]]：[[#koutokui|幸徳井世系考訂本]] == 登場作品 == * {{Cite book|和書|author=荒山徹|authorlink=荒山徹|title=柳生陰陽剣|editor=|publisher=|year=2008|isbn=978-4-10-121042-1}}『柳生雨月抄』改題 - [[幸徳井友景]]（柳生友景）を[[主人公]]とした[[伝奇小説]]。 * [[あざの耕平]] 『[[東京レイヴンズ]]』 - [[陰陽師]]を題材とした[[ライトノベル]]。第二部にサブキャラとして幸徳井家の陰陽師が登場する。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{notelist2}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite journal|和書|author=中尾堯 編|title=幸徳井家と南都陰陽道|editor=|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|journal=中世の寺院体制と社会|year=2002|isbn=464202817X}} * {{オープンアクセス}}{{Cite book|和書|url=https://webarchives.tnm.jp/dlib/detail/839|title=幸徳井世系考訂本|publisher=（明治期・筆記史料）}} * {{Cite web|url=https://miko.org/~uraki/kuon/furu/chara/kamo_keifu.htm|author=F.O.G.|title=賀茂家系圖　幸德井‧勘解由小路|accessdate=2019-10-11}} == 関連項目 == * [[鎮宅霊符神社]] - 奈良市陰陽町、南都陰陽師の拠点 * [[吉備塚古墳]] * [[奈良教育大学]] * [[南都鏡神社]] * [[奈良市立飛鳥小学校]]（幸町付近） * [[社家町]] * [[閼伽井庵]]（旧・高畠村[[閼伽井]]） * [[飛鳥神社]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こうとくいけ}} [[Category:幸徳井家|!]] [[Category:賀茂朝臣氏|@こうとくいけ]] [[Category:日本の氏族]] {{Refimprove|date=October 2019}} {{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Kōtokui family | crest = Unknown.jpg | crest_caption = | main_house = {{ill|Kamo no Asomi clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} (branch family) | founder = {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoyuki|ja|幸徳井友幸}} | type = {{ill|Jige-ke|ja|地下家}}
{{ill|Shizoku|ja|士族}} | origin = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province}} Kōtokui in Takahata Village | residence = Kōtokui, Takahata Village, Yamato Province
Present-day {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}} Takahatachō and Onyōchō | notable_members = {{ill|Kōtokui Tomokage|ja|幸徳井友景}} }} '''Kōtokui family''' (幸徳井家) refers to a {{ill|Jige-ke|ja|地下家}} based in {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良}}, engaged in {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}} as their hereditary occupation. Though originally descendants of the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏}}, the family became associated with the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏}} after {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoyuki|ja|幸徳井友幸}}, the founder, was adopted by the Kamo clan. {{see also|Kamo clan|Kadenokōji family (Kamo clan)}} == Overview == In 1391, Tomoyuki was born as the second son of {{ill|Abe no Tomouji|ja|安倍友氏}}, a branch family of the Abe clan. Tomoyuki studied {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}} under {{ill|Kamo no Sadahiro|ja|賀茂定弘}}, who served as {{ill|Gyōbu-kyō|ja|刑部卿}} and {{ill|Onmyōjō|ja|陰陽助}}. In 1419, Tomoyuki was adopted by Sadahiro and took the name Kamo no Tomoyuki. He rose to the Rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}} by 1463 and established his residence in Kōtokui, a well-known area near {{ill|Takahata Village|ja|高畠村}}, serving as a political advisor to {{ill|Jinson|ja|尋尊}} of {{ill|Daijōin Temple|ja|大乗院}}. The family name "Kōtokui" derives from this location. After Tomoyuki's death in 1473, his descendants continued to serve Daijōin Temple and played key roles in supervising {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}} and {{ill|Shōmonshi|ja|声聞師}} in the Nara region, even compiling their own calendar, the {{ill|Kōtokui Calendar|ja|幸徳井暦}}. === Edo Period === In the early Edo period, {{ill|Tsuchimikado Yasuhiro|ja|土御門泰重}} resigned from the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료}} due to repeated failures, leaving a vacancy for {{ill|Onmyō no Kami|ja|陰陽頭}}. With the main Kamo family line extinct, {{ill|Kōtokui Tomokage|ja|幸徳井友景}}, the ninth head of the Kōtokui family, was appointed Onmyō no Kami in 1618. This appointment led to a series of Kōtokui family members, such as {{ill|Kōtokui Tomotane|ja|幸徳井友種}} and {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoden|ja|幸徳井友傳}}, succeeding to this position. However, by the late 17th century, the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}} regained the title of Onmyō no Kami and stripped the Kōtokui family of their control over southern Nara's Onmyōji. Despite this, the Kōtokui family maintained influence over Nara temples and continued advising on temple rituals. === Meiji Period and Beyond === Following the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the Kōtokui family petitioned to be ennobled as {{ill|Kazoku|ja|華族}} due to their historical contributions. The petition was denied, and their activities after this period remain unknown. == Heads of the Kōtokui Family == 1. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoyuki|ja|幸徳井友幸}} (1391–1473) - Senior Third Rank 2. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoshige|ja|幸徳井友重}} (1411–1490) - Senior Third Rank 3. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomonobu|ja|幸徳井友延}} (1435–1494) - Junior Third Rank ... 24. {{ill|Kōtokui Yasuhiko|ja|幸徳井保章}} (1847–?) == References in Literature == * {{Cite book|author=Arayama Toru|title=Yagyū Onmyōken|year=2008|isbn=978-4-10-121042-1}} * {{ill|Azano Kōhei|ja|あざの耕平}}, ''Tokyo Ravens'' - A {{ill|Light novel|ja|ライトノベル}} series featuring Onmyōji, including Kōtokui family members in a supporting role. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Chintaku Reifu Shrine|ja|鎮宅霊符神社}} * {{ill|Nara Educational University|ja|奈良教育大学}} * {{ill|Asuka Shrine (Nara)|ja|飛鳥神社}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kōtokui Family]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kōtokui family|!]] [[Category:Kamo no Asomi clan|@]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Kinki region clans]] [[Category:Kuge]] [[Category:Onmyōryō]] [[Category:Onmyōdō]] [[Category:History of Nara]] [[Category:Sōjō District]] [[Category:Japanese-history-stub]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:幸徳井家]] [[sn:Kōtokui Family]] [[en:Kōtokui Family]] [[Category:Ambox transclusions]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kōtokui Family]][[Category:qq]] {{translated page|ja|幸徳井家|version=100215448|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{Refimprove|date=October 2019}} {{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Kōtokui family | crest = Unknown.jpg | crest_caption = | main_house = {{ill|Kamo no Asomi clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=draft}} (branch family) | founder = {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoyuki|ja|幸徳井友幸|12=draft}} | type = {{ill|Jige-ke|ja|地下家|12=draft}}

Present-day {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市|12=draft}} Takahatachō and Onyōchō | notable_members = {{ill|Kōtokui Tomokage|ja|幸徳井友景|12=draft}} }} '''Kōtokui family''' (幸徳井家) refers to a {{ill|Jige-ke|ja|地下家|12=draft}} based in {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良|12=draft}}, engaged in {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道|12=draft}} as their hereditary occupation. Though originally descendants of the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏|12=draft}}, the family became associated with the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=draft}} after {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoyuki|ja|幸徳井友幸|12=draft}}, the founder, was adopted by the Kamo clan. {{see also|Kamo clan|Kadenokōji family (Kamo clan)}} == Overview == In 1391, Tomoyuki was born as the second son of {{ill|Abe no Tomouji|ja|安倍友氏|12=draft}}, a branch family of the Abe clan. Tomoyuki studied {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道|12=draft}} under {{ill|Kamo no Sadahiro|ja|賀茂定弘|12=draft}}, who served as {{ill|Gyōbu-kyō|ja|刑部卿|12=draft}} and {{ill|Onmyōjō|ja|陰陽助|12=draft}}. In 1419, Tomoyuki was adopted by Sadahiro and took the name Kamo no Tomoyuki. He rose to the Rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|12=draft}} by 1463 and established his residence in Kōtokui, a well-known area near {{ill|Takahata Village|ja|高畠村|12=draft}}, serving as a political advisor to {{ill|Jinson|ja|尋尊|12=draft}} of {{ill|Daijōin Temple|ja|大乗院|12=draft}}. The family name "Kōtokui" derives from this location. After Tomoyuki's death in 1473, his descendants continued to serve Daijōin Temple and played key roles in supervising {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師|12=draft}} and {{ill|Shōmonshi|ja|声聞師|12=draft}} in the Nara region, even compiling their own calendar, the {{ill|Kōtokui Calendar|ja|幸徳井暦|12=draft}}. === Edo Period === In the early Edo period, {{ill|Tsuchimikado Yasuhiro|ja|土御門泰重|12=draft}} resigned from the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=draft}} due to repeated failures, leaving a vacancy for {{ill|Onmyō no Kami|ja|陰陽頭|12=draft}}. With the main Kamo family line extinct, {{ill|Kōtokui Tomokage|ja|幸徳井友景|12=draft}}, the ninth head of the Kōtokui family, was appointed Onmyō no Kami in 1618. This appointment led to a series of Kōtokui family members, such as {{ill|Kōtokui Tomotane|ja|幸徳井友種|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoden|ja|幸徳井友傳|12=draft}}, succeeding to this position. However, by the late 17th century, the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=draft}} regained the title of Onmyō no Kami and stripped the Kōtokui family of their control over southern Nara's Onmyōji. Despite this, the Kōtokui family maintained influence over Nara temples and continued advising on temple rituals. === Meiji Period and Beyond === Following the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|12=draft}}, the Kōtokui family petitioned to be ennobled as {{ill|Kazoku|ja|華族|12=draft}} due to their historical contributions. The petition was denied, and their activities after this period remain unknown. == Heads of the Kōtokui Family == 1. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoyuki|ja|幸徳井友幸|12=draft}} (1391–1473) - Senior Third Rank 2. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoshige|ja|幸徳井友重|12=draft}} (1411–1490) - Senior Third Rank 3. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomonobu|ja|幸徳井友延|12=draft}} (1435–1494) - Junior Third Rank ... 24. {{ill|Kōtokui Yasuhiko|ja|幸徳井保章|12=draft}} (1847–?) == References in Literature == * {{Cite book|author=Arayama Toru|title=Yagyū Onmyōken|year=2008|isbn=978-4-10-121042-1}} * {{ill|Azano Kōhei|ja|あざの耕平|12=draft}}, ''Tokyo Ravens'' - A {{ill|Light novel|ja|ライトノベル|12=draft}} series featuring Onmyōji, including Kōtokui family members in a supporting role. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Chintaku Reifu Shrine|ja|鎮宅霊符神社|12=draft}} * {{ill|Nara Educational University|ja|奈良教育大学|12=draft}} * {{ill|Asuka Shrine (Nara)|ja|飛鳥神社|12=draft}} {{translated page|ja|幸徳井家|version=100215448|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kōtokui Family]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kōtokui family|!]] [[Category:Kamo no Asomi clan|@]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Kinki region clans]] [[Category:Kuge]] [[Category:Onmyōryō]] [[Category:Onmyōdō]] [[Category:History of Nara]] [[Category:Sōjō District]] [[Category:Japanese-history-stub]] [[Category:Ambox transclusions]] }} [[ja:幸徳井家]] [[sn:Kōtokui Family]] [[en:Kōtokui Family]]

{{Refimprove|date=October 2019}} {{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Kōtokui family | crest = Unknown.jpg | crest_caption = | main_house = {{ill|Kamo no Asomi clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|qq=draft}} (branch family) | founder = {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoyuki|ja|幸徳井友幸|qq=draft}} | type = {{ill|Jige-ke|ja|地下家|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Shizoku|ja|士族|qq=draft}} | origin = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|qq=draft}} Kōtokui in Takahata Village | residence = Kōtokui, Takahata Village, Yamato Province
Present-day {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市|qq=draft}} Takahatachō and Onyōchō | notable_members = {{ill|Kōtokui Tomokage|ja|幸徳井友景|qq=draft}} }} '''Kōtokui family''' (幸徳井家) refers to a {{ill|Jige-ke|ja|地下家|qq=draft}} based in {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良|qq=draft}}, engaged in {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道|qq=draft}} as their hereditary occupation. Though originally descendants of the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏|qq=draft}}, the family became associated with the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|qq=draft}} after {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoyuki|ja|幸徳井友幸|qq=draft}}, the founder, was adopted by the Kamo clan. {{see also|Kamo clan|Kadenokōji family (Kamo clan)}} == Overview == In 1391, Tomoyuki was born as the second son of {{ill|Abe no Tomouji|ja|安倍友氏|qq=draft}}, a branch family of the Abe clan. Tomoyuki studied {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道|qq=draft}} under {{ill|Kamo no Sadahiro|ja|賀茂定弘|qq=draft}}, who served as {{ill|Gyōbu-kyō|ja|刑部卿|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Onmyōjō|ja|陰陽助|qq=draft}}. In 1419, Tomoyuki was adopted by Sadahiro and took the name Kamo no Tomoyuki. He rose to the Rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft}} by 1463 and established his residence in Kōtokui, a well-known area near {{ill|Takahata Village|ja|高畠村|qq=draft}}, serving as a political advisor to {{ill|Jinson|ja|尋尊|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Daijōin Temple|ja|大乗院|qq=draft}}. The family name "Kōtokui" derives from this location. After Tomoyuki's death in 1473, his descendants continued to serve Daijōin Temple and played key roles in supervising {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Shōmonshi|ja|声聞師|qq=draft}} in the Nara region, even compiling their own calendar, the {{ill|Kōtokui Calendar|ja|幸徳井暦|qq=draft}}. === Edo Period === In the early Edo period, {{ill|Tsuchimikado Yasuhiro|ja|土御門泰重|qq=draft}} resigned from the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|qq=draft}} due to repeated failures, leaving a vacancy for {{ill|Onmyō no Kami|ja|陰陽頭|qq=draft}}. With the main Kamo family line extinct, {{ill|Kōtokui Tomokage|ja|幸徳井友景|qq=draft}}, the ninth head of the Kōtokui family, was appointed Onmyō no Kami in 1618. This appointment led to a series of Kōtokui family members, such as {{ill|Kōtokui Tomotane|ja|幸徳井友種|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoden|ja|幸徳井友傳|qq=draft}}, succeeding to this position. However, by the late 17th century, the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|qq=draft}} regained the title of Onmyō no Kami and stripped the Kōtokui family of their control over southern Nara's Onmyōji. Despite this, the Kōtokui family maintained influence over Nara temples and continued advising on temple rituals. === Meiji Period and Beyond === Following the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|qq=draft}}, the Kōtokui family petitioned to be ennobled as {{ill|Kazoku|ja|華族|qq=draft}} due to their historical contributions. The petition was denied, and their activities after this period remain unknown. == Heads of the Kōtokui Family == 1. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoyuki|ja|幸徳井友幸|qq=draft}} (1391–1473) - Senior Third Rank 2. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoshige|ja|幸徳井友重|qq=draft}} (1411–1490) - Senior Third Rank 3. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomonobu|ja|幸徳井友延|qq=draft}} (1435–1494) - Junior Third Rank ... 24. {{ill|Kōtokui Yasuhiko|ja|幸徳井保章|qq=draft}} (1847–?) == References in Literature == * {{Cite book|author=Arayama Toru|title=Yagyū Onmyōken|year=2008|isbn=978-4-10-121042-1}} * {{ill|Azano Kōhei|ja|あざの耕平|qq=draft}}, ''Tokyo Ravens'' - A {{ill|Light novel|ja|ライトノベル|qq=draft}} series featuring Onmyōji, including Kōtokui family members in a supporting role. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Chintaku Reifu Shrine|ja|鎮宅霊符神社|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Nara Educational University|ja|奈良教育大学|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Asuka Shrine (Nara)|ja|飛鳥神社|qq=draft}} {{translated page|ja|幸徳井家|version=100215448|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kōtokui Family]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kōtokui family|!]] [[Category:Kamo no Asomi clan|@]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Kinki region clans]] [[Category:Kuge]] [[Category:Onmyōryō]] [[Category:Onmyōdō]] [[Category:History of Nara]] [[Category:Sōjō District]] [[Category:Japanese-history-stub]] [[Category:Ambox transclusions]] }} [[ja:幸徳井家]] [[sn:Kōtokui Family]] [[en:Kōtokui Family]]

revision.text:
{{Refimprove|date=October 2019}} {{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Kōtokui family | crest = Unknown.jpg | crest_caption = | main_house = {{ill|Kamo no Asomi clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Asomi clan|qq=}} (branch family) | founder = {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoyuki|ja|幸徳井友幸|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomoyuki|qq=}} | type = {{ill|Jige-ke|ja|地下家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jige-ke|qq=}}
{{ill|Shizoku|ja|士族|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizoku|qq=}} | origin = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国|qq=Yamato Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Province|qq=}} Kōtokui in Takahata Village | residence = Kōtokui, Takahata Village, Yamato Province
Present-day {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara|qq=}} Takahatachō and Onyōchō | notable_members = {{ill|Kōtokui Tomokage|ja|幸徳井友景|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomokage|qq=}} }} '''Kōtokui family''' (幸徳井家) refers to a {{ill|Jige-ke|ja|地下家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jige-ke|qq=}} based in {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara|qq=}}, engaged in {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōdō|qq=}} as their hereditary occupation. Though originally descendants of the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|qq=}}, the family became associated with the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}} after {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoyuki|ja|幸徳井友幸|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomoyuki|qq=}}, the founder, was adopted by the Kamo clan. {{see also|Kamo clan|Kadenokōji family (Kamo clan)}} == Overview == In 1391, Tomoyuki was born as the second son of {{ill|Abe no Tomouji|ja|安倍友氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Tomouji|qq=}}, a branch family of the Abe clan. Tomoyuki studied {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōdō|qq=}} under {{ill|Kamo no Sadahiro|ja|賀茂定弘|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Sadahiro|qq=}}, who served as {{ill|Gyōbu-kyō|ja|刑部卿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōbu-kyō|qq=}} and {{ill|Onmyōjō|ja|陰陽助|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōjō|qq=}}. In 1419, Tomoyuki was adopted by Sadahiro and took the name Kamo no Tomoyuki. He rose to the Rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}} by 1463 and established his residence in Kōtokui, a well-known area near {{ill|Takahata Village|ja|高畠村|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahata Village|qq=}}, serving as a political advisor to {{ill|Jinson|ja|尋尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinson|qq=}} of {{ill|Daijōin Temple|ja|大乗院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōin Temple|qq=}}. The family name "Kōtokui" derives from this location. After Tomoyuki's death in 1473, his descendants continued to serve Daijōin Temple and played key roles in supervising {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōji|qq=}} and {{ill|Shōmonshi|ja|声聞師|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōmonshi|qq=}} in the Nara region, even compiling their own calendar, the {{ill|Kōtokui Calendar|ja|幸徳井暦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Calendar|qq=}}. === Edo Period === In the early Edo period, {{ill|Tsuchimikado Yasuhiro|ja|土御門泰重|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Yasuhiro|qq=}} resigned from the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}} due to repeated failures, leaving a vacancy for {{ill|Onmyō no Kami|ja|陰陽頭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō no Kami|qq=}}. With the main Kamo family line extinct, {{ill|Kōtokui Tomokage|ja|幸徳井友景|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomokage|qq=}}, the ninth head of the Kōtokui family, was appointed Onmyō no Kami in 1618. This appointment led to a series of Kōtokui family members, such as {{ill|Kōtokui Tomotane|ja|幸徳井友種|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomotane|qq=}} and {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoden|ja|幸徳井友傳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomoden|qq=}}, succeeding to this position. However, by the late 17th century, the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}} regained the title of Onmyō no Kami and stripped the Kōtokui family of their control over southern Nara's Onmyōji. Despite this, the Kōtokui family maintained influence over Nara temples and continued advising on temple rituals. === Meiji Period and Beyond === Following the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Restoration|qq=}}, the Kōtokui family petitioned to be ennobled as {{ill|Kazoku|ja|華族|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazoku|qq=}} due to their historical contributions. The petition was denied, and their activities after this period remain unknown. == Heads of the Kōtokui Family == 1. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoyuki|ja|幸徳井友幸|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomoyuki|qq=}} (1391–1473) - Senior Third Rank 2. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoshige|ja|幸徳井友重|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomoshige|qq=}} (1411–1490) - Senior Third Rank 3. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomonobu|ja|幸徳井友延|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomonobu|qq=}} (1435–1494) - Junior Third Rank ... 24. {{ill|Kōtokui Yasuhiko|ja|幸徳井保章|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Yasuhiko|qq=}} (1847–?) == References in Literature == * {{Cite book|author=Arayama Toru|title=Yagyū Onmyōken|year=2008|isbn=978-4-10-121042-1}} * {{ill|Azano Kōhei|ja|あざの耕平|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azano Kōhei|qq=}}, ''Tokyo Ravens'' - A {{ill|Light novel|ja|ライトノベル|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Light novel|qq=}} series featuring Onmyōji, including Kōtokui family members in a supporting role. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Chintaku Reifu Shrine|ja|鎮宅霊符神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chintaku Reifu Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Nara Educational University|ja|奈良教育大学|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Educational University|qq=}} * {{ill|Asuka Shrine (Nara)|ja|飛鳥神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka Shrine (Nara)|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|幸徳井家|version=100215448|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kōtokui Family]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kōtokui family|!]] [[Category:Kamo no Asomi clan|@]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Kinki region clans]] [[Category:Kuge]] [[Category:Onmyōryō]] [[Category:Onmyōdō]] [[Category:History of Nara]] [[Category:Sōjō District]] [[Category:Japanese-history-stub]] }} [[ja:幸徳井家]] [[sn:Kōtokui Family]] [[en:Kōtokui Family]]

However, by the late 17th century, the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}} regained the title of Onmyō no Kami and stripped the Kōtokui family of their control over southern Nara's Onmyōji. The petition was denied, and their activities after this period remain unknown. == Heads of the Kōtokui Family == 1. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoyuki|ja|幸徳井友幸|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomoyuki|qq=}} (1391–1473) - Senior Third Rank 2. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoshige|ja|幸徳井友重|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomoshige|qq=}} (1411–1490) - Senior Third Rank 3. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomonobu|ja|幸徳井友延|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomonobu|qq=}} (1435–1494) - Junior Third Rank ... 24. {{ill|Kōtokui Yasuhiko|ja|幸徳井保章|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Yasuhiko|qq=}} (1847–?) == References in Literature == * {{Cite book|author=Arayama Toru|title=Yagyū Onmyōken|year=2008|isbn=978-4-10-121042-1}} * {{ill|Azano Kōhei|ja|あざの耕平|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azano Kōhei|qq=}}, ''Tokyo Ravens'' - A {{ill|Light novel|ja|ライトノベル|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Light novel|qq=}} series featuring Onmyōji, including Kōtokui family members in a supporting role. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Chintaku Reifu Shrine|ja|鎮宅霊符神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chintaku Reifu Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Nara Educational University|ja|奈良教育大学|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Educational University|qq=}} * {{ill|Asuka Shrine (Nara)|ja|飛鳥神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka Shrine (Nara)|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|幸徳井家|version=100215448|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kamo no Asomi clan|@]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Kinki region clans]] [[Category:Kuge]] [[Category:Onmyōryō]] [[Category:Onmyōdō]] [[Category:History of Nara]] [[Category:Japanese-history-stub]] }} [[ja:幸徳井家]] [[sn:Kōtokui Family]] [[en:Kōtokui Family]]

{{Refimprove|date=October 2019}} {{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Kōtokui family | crest = Unknown.jpg | crest_caption = | main_house = {{ill|Kamo no Asomi clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Asomi clan|qq=}} (branch family) | founder = {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoyuki|ja|幸徳井友幸|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomoyuki|qq=}} | type = {{ill|Jige-ke|ja|地下家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jige-ke|qq=}}
{{ill|Shizoku|ja|士族|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizoku|qq=}} | origin = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国|qq=Yamato Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Province|qq=}} Kōtokui in Takahata Village | residence = Kōtokui, Takahata Village, Yamato Province
Present-day {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara|qq=}} Takahatachō and Onyōchō | notable_members = {{ill|Kōtokui Tomokage|ja|幸徳井友景|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomokage|qq=}} }} '''Kōtokui family''' (幸徳井家) refers to a {{ill|Jige-ke|ja|地下家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jige-ke|qq=}} based in {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara|qq=}}, engaged in {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōdō|qq=}} as their hereditary occupation. Though originally descendants of the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|qq=}}, the family became associated with the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}} after {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoyuki|ja|幸徳井友幸|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomoyuki|qq=}}, the founder, was adopted by the Kamo clan. {{see also|Kamo clan|Kadenokōji family (Kamo clan)}} == Overview == In 1391, Tomoyuki was born as the second son of {{ill|Abe no Tomouji|ja|安倍友氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Tomouji|qq=}}, a branch family of the Abe clan. Tomoyuki studied {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōdō|qq=}} under {{ill|Kamo no Sadahiro|ja|賀茂定弘|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Sadahiro|qq=}}, who served as {{ill|Gyōbu-kyō|ja|刑部卿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōbu-kyō|qq=}} and {{ill|Onmyōjō|ja|陰陽助|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōjō|qq=}}. In 1419, Tomoyuki was adopted by Sadahiro and took the name Kamo no Tomoyuki. He rose to the Rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}} by 1463 and established his residence in Kōtokui, a well-known area near {{ill|Takahata Village|ja|高畠村|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahata Village|qq=}}, serving as a political advisor to {{ill|Jinson|ja|尋尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinson|qq=}} of {{ill|Daijōin Temple|ja|大乗院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōin Temple|qq=}}. The family name "Kōtokui" derives from this location. After Tomoyuki's death in 1473, his descendants continued to serve Daijōin Temple and played key roles in supervising {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōji|qq=}} and {{ill|Shōmonshi|ja|声聞師|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōmonshi|qq=}} in the Nara region, even compiling their own calendar, the {{ill|Kōtokui Calendar|ja|幸徳井暦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Calendar|qq=}}. === Edo Period === In the early Edo period, {{ill|Tsuchimikado Yasuhiro|ja|土御門泰重|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Yasuhiro|qq=}} resigned from the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}} due to repeated failures, leaving a vacancy for {{ill|Onmyō no Kami|ja|陰陽頭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō no Kami|qq=}}. With the main Kamo family line extinct, {{ill|Kōtokui Tomokage|ja|幸徳井友景|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomokage|qq=}}, the ninth head of the Kōtokui family, was appointed Onmyō no Kami in 1618. This appointment led to a series of Kōtokui family members, such as {{ill|Kōtokui Tomotane|ja|幸徳井友種|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomotane|qq=}} and {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoden|ja|幸徳井友傳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomoden|qq=}}, succeeding to this position. However, by the late 17th century, the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}} regained the title of Onmyō no Kami and stripped the Kōtokui family of their control over southern Nara's Onmyōji. Despite this, the Kōtokui family maintained influence over Nara temples and continued advising on temple rituals. === Meiji Period and Beyond === Following the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Restoration|qq=}}, the Kōtokui family petitioned to be ennobled as {{ill|Kazoku|ja|華族|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazoku|qq=}} due to their historical contributions. The petition was denied, and their activities after this period remain unknown. == Heads of the Kōtokui Family == 1. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoyuki|ja|幸徳井友幸|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomoyuki|qq=}} (1391–1473) - Senior Third Rank 2. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoshige|ja|幸徳井友重|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomoshige|qq=}} (1411–1490) - Senior Third Rank 3. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomonobu|ja|幸徳井友延|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomonobu|qq=}} (1435–1494) - Junior Third Rank ... 24. {{ill|Kōtokui Yasuhiko|ja|幸徳井保章|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Yasuhiko|qq=}} (1847–?) == References in Literature == * {{Cite book|author=Arayama Toru|title=Yagyū Onmyōken|year=2008|isbn=978-4-10-121042-1}} * {{ill|Azano Kōhei|ja|あざの耕平|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azano Kōhei|qq=}}, ''Tokyo Ravens'' - A {{ill|Light novel|ja|ライトノベル|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Light novel|qq=}} series featuring Onmyōji, including Kōtokui family members in a supporting role. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Chintaku Reifu Shrine|ja|鎮宅霊符神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chintaku Reifu Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Nara Educational University|ja|奈良教育大学|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Educational University|qq=}} * {{ill|Asuka Shrine (Nara)|ja|飛鳥神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka Shrine (Nara)|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|幸徳井家|version=100215448|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kamo no Asomi clan|@]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Kinki region clans]] [[Category:Kuge]] [[Category:Onmyōryō]] [[Category:Onmyōdō]] [[Category:History of Nara]] [[Category:Japanese-history-stub]] }} [[ja:幸徳井家]] [[sn:Kōtokui Family]] [[en:Kōtokui Family]]
