After Tomoyuki's death in 1473, his descendants continued to serve Daijōin Temple and played key roles in supervising [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]] and {{ill|Shōmonshi|ja|声聞師|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōmonshi|qq=}} in the Nara region, even compiling their own calendar, the {{ill|Kōtokui Calendar|ja|幸徳井暦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Calendar|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 南都暦}}. === Edo Period === In the early Edo period, {{ill|Tsuchimikado Yasuhiro|ja|土御門泰重|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Yasuhiro|qq=|1=Yasushige Tsuchimikado|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423489}} resigned from the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}} due to repeated failures, leaving a vacancy for {{ill|Onmyō no Kami|ja|陰陽頭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō no Kami|qq=|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}}. With the main Kamo family line extinct, {{ill|Kōtokui Tomokage|ja|幸徳井友景|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomokage|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the ninth head of the Kōtokui family, was appointed Onmyō no Kami in 1618. This appointment led to a series of Kōtokui family members, such as {{ill|Kōtokui Tomotane|ja|幸徳井友種|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomotane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoden|ja|幸徳井友傳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomoden|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, succeeding to this position. However, by the late 17th century, the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}} regained the title of Onmyō no Kami and stripped the Kōtokui family of their control over southern Nara's Onmyōji. Despite this, the Kōtokui family maintained influence over Nara temples and continued advising on temple rituals. === Meiji Period and Beyond === Following the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the Kōtokui family petitioned to be ennobled as [[:en:Kazoku|Kazoku]] due to their historical contributions. The petition was denied, and their activities after this period remain unknown. == Heads of the Kōtokui Family == 1. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoyuki|ja|幸徳井友幸|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomoyuki|qq=|1=Kōtokui Tomoyuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q102247147}} (1391–1473) - Senior Third Rank 2. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoshige|ja|幸徳井友重|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomoshige|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1411–1490) - Senior Third Rank 3. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomonobu|ja|幸徳井友延|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomonobu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1435–1494) - Junior Third Rank ... 24. {{ill|Kōtokui Yasuhiko|ja|幸徳井保章|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Yasuhiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1847–?) == References in Literature == * {{Cite book|author=Arayama Toru|title=Yagyū Onmyōken|year=2008|isbn=978-4-10-121042-1}} * {{ill|Azano Kōhei|ja|あざの耕平|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azano Kōhei|qq=|1=Kōhei Azano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11257270}}, ''Tokyo Ravens'' - A [[:en:Light novel|Light novel]] series featuring Onmyōji, including Kōtokui family members in a supporting role. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Chintaku Reifu Shrine|ja|鎮宅霊符神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chintaku Reifu Shrine|qq=}} * [[:en:Nara University of Education|Nara Educational University]] * {{ill|Kyobate Tenjinsha|ja|飛鳥神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyobate Tenjinsha|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|幸徳井家|version=100215448|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Kuge]] [[Category:Onmyōdō]] [[Category:History of Nara]] }} [[ja:幸徳井家]] [[Category:History of Nara]] [[Category:Kōtokuji Family]] [[Category:Soeage District]] [[Category:Kamo no Asomi Clan]] [[Category:Clans by region]] [[Category:Onmyōryō]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] {{wikidata link|Q11483987}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11483987]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:50, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Refimprove|date=October 2019}} {{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Kōtokui family | crest = Unknown.jpg | crest_caption = | main_house = {{ill|Kamo no Asomi clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Asomi clan|qq=|1=Kamo no Ason clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q122979457}} (branch family) | founder = {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoyuki|ja|幸徳井友幸|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomoyuki|qq=|1=Kōtokui Tomoyuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q102247147}} | type = {{ill|Jige-ke|ja|地下家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jige-ke|qq=|1=Jigeke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424052}}
[[:en:Shizoku|Shizoku]] | origin = [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] Kōtokui in Takahata Village | residence = Kōtokui, Takahata Village, Yamato Province
Present-day [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]] Takahatachō and Onyōchō | notable_members = {{ill|Kōtokui Tomokage|ja|幸徳井友景|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomokage|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} }} '''Kōtokui family''' (幸徳井家) refers to a {{ill|Jige-ke|ja|地下家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jige-ke|qq=|1=Jigeke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424052}} based in {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara|qq=}}, engaged in [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]] as their hereditary occupation. Though originally descendants of the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|qq=|1=Abe clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657247}}, the family became associated with the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}} after {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoyuki|ja|幸徳井友幸|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomoyuki|qq=|1=Kōtokui Tomoyuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q102247147}}, the founder, was adopted by the Kamo clan. {{see also|Kamo clan|Kadenokōji family (Kamo clan)}} == Overview == In 1391, Tomoyuki was born as the second son of {{ill|Abe no Tomouji|ja|安倍友氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Tomouji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a branch family of the Abe clan. Tomoyuki studied [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]] under {{ill|Kamo no Sadahiro|ja|賀茂定弘|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Sadahiro|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who served as {{ill|Gyōbu-kyō|ja|刑部卿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōbu-kyō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 刑部省|1=Ministry of Justice|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6867320}} and {{ill|Onmyōjō|ja|陰陽助|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōjō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 陰陽寮|1=Onmyō-ryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658260}}. In 1419, Tomoyuki was adopted by Sadahiro and took the name Kamo no Tomoyuki. He rose to the Rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}} by 1463 and established his residence in Kōtokui, a well-known area near {{ill|Takahata Village|ja|高畠村|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahata Village|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|1=Takahata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q772103}}, serving as a political advisor to {{ill|Jinson|ja|尋尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinson|qq=|1=Jinson|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7138674}} of {{ill|Daijōin Temple|ja|大乗院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōin Temple|qq=|1=Daijō-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16766994}}. The family name "Kōtokui" derives from this location. After Tomoyuki's death in 1473, his descendants continued to serve Daijōin Temple and played key roles in supervising [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]] and {{ill|Shōmonshi|ja|声聞師|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōmonshi|qq=}} in the Nara region, even compiling their own calendar, the {{ill|Kōtokui Calendar|ja|幸徳井暦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Calendar|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 南都暦}}. === Edo Period === In the early Edo period, {{ill|Tsuchimikado Yasuhiro|ja|土御門泰重|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Yasuhiro|qq=|1=Yasushige Tsuchimikado|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423489}} resigned from the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}} due to repeated failures, leaving a vacancy for {{ill|Onmyō no Kami|ja|陰陽頭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō no Kami|qq=|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}}. With the main Kamo family line extinct, {{ill|Kōtokui Tomokage|ja|幸徳井友景|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomokage|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the ninth head of the Kōtokui family, was appointed Onmyō no Kami in 1618. This appointment led to a series of Kōtokui family members, such as {{ill|Kōtokui Tomotane|ja|幸徳井友種|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomotane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoden|ja|幸徳井友傳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomoden|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, succeeding to this position. However, by the late 17th century, the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}} regained the title of Onmyō no Kami and stripped the Kōtokui family of their control over southern Nara's Onmyōji. Despite this, the Kōtokui family maintained influence over Nara temples and continued advising on temple rituals. === Meiji Period and Beyond === Following the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the Kōtokui family petitioned to be ennobled as [[:en:Kazoku|Kazoku]] due to their historical contributions. The petition was denied, and their activities after this period remain unknown. == Heads of the Kōtokui Family == 1. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoyuki|ja|幸徳井友幸|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomoyuki|qq=|1=Kōtokui Tomoyuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q102247147}} (1391–1473) - Senior Third Rank 2. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoshige|ja|幸徳井友重|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomoshige|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1411–1490) - Senior Third Rank 3. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomonobu|ja|幸徳井友延|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomonobu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1435–1494) - Junior Third Rank ... 24. {{ill|Kōtokui Yasuhiko|ja|幸徳井保章|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Yasuhiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1847–?) == References in Literature == * {{Cite book|author=Arayama Toru|title=Yagyū Onmyōken|year=2008|isbn=978-4-10-121042-1}} * {{ill|Azano Kōhei|ja|あざの耕平|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azano Kōhei|qq=|1=Kōhei Azano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11257270}}, ''Tokyo Ravens'' - A [[:en:Light novel|Light novel]] series featuring Onmyōji, including Kōtokui family members in a supporting role. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Chintaku Reifu Shrine|ja|鎮宅霊符神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chintaku Reifu Shrine|qq=}} * [[:en:Nara University of Education|Nara Educational University]] * {{ill|Kyobate Tenjinsha|ja|飛鳥神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyobate Tenjinsha|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|幸徳井家|version=100215448|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Kuge]] [[Category:Onmyōdō]] [[Category:History of Nara]] }} [[ja:幸徳井家]] [[Category:History of Nara]] [[Category:Kōtokuji Family]] [[Category:Soeage District]] [[Category:Kamo no Asomi Clan]] [[Category:Clans by region]] [[Category:Onmyōryō]] [[Category:qq]] {{wikidata link|Q11483987}}

Despite this, the Kōtokui family maintained influence over Nara temples and continued advising on temple rituals. === Meiji Period and Beyond === Following the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the Kōtokui family petitioned to be ennobled as [[:en:Kazoku|Kazoku]] due to their historical contributions. The petition was denied, and their activities after this period remain unknown. == Heads of the Kōtokui Family == 1. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoyuki|ja|幸徳井友幸|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomoyuki|qq=|1=Kōtokui Tomoyuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q102247147}} (1391–1473) - Senior Third Rank 2. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoshige|ja|幸徳井友重|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomoshige|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1411–1490) - Senior Third Rank 3. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomonobu|ja|幸徳井友延|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomonobu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1435–1494) - Junior Third Rank ... 24. {{ill|Kōtokui Yasuhiko|ja|幸徳井保章|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Yasuhiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1847–?) == References in Literature == * {{Cite book|author=Arayama Toru|title=Yagyū Onmyōken|year=2008|isbn=978-4-10-121042-1}} * {{ill|Azano Kōhei|ja|あざの耕平|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azano Kōhei|qq=|1=Kōhei Azano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11257270}}, ''Tokyo Ravens'' - A [[:en:Light novel|Light novel]] series featuring Onmyōji, including Kōtokui family members in a supporting role. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Chintaku Reifu Shrine|ja|鎮宅霊符神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chintaku Reifu Shrine|qq=}} * [[:en:Nara University of Education|Nara Educational University]] * {{ill|Kyobate Tenjinsha|ja|飛鳥神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyobate Tenjinsha|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|幸徳井家|version=100215448|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Kuge]] [[Category:Onmyōdō]] [[Category:History of Nara]] }} [[ja:幸徳井家]] [[Category:History of Nara]] [[Category:Kōtokuji Family]] [[Category:Soeage District]] [[Category:Kamo no Asomi Clan]] [[Category:Clans by region]] [[Category:Onmyōryō]] {{wikidata link|Q11483987}}

[[ja:幸徳井家]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11483987]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:50, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Refimprove|date=October 2019}} {{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Kōtokui family | crest = Unknown.jpg | crest_caption = | main_house = {{ill|Kamo no Asomi clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Asomi clan|qq=|1=Kamo no Ason clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q122979457}} (branch family) | founder = {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoyuki|ja|幸徳井友幸|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomoyuki|qq=|1=Kōtokui Tomoyuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q102247147}} | type = {{ill|Jige-ke|ja|地下家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jige-ke|qq=|1=Jigeke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424052}}
[[:en:Shizoku|Shizoku]] | origin = [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] Kōtokui in Takahata Village | residence = Kōtokui, Takahata Village, Yamato Province
Present-day [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]] Takahatachō and Onyōchō | notable_members = {{ill|Kōtokui Tomokage|ja|幸徳井友景|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomokage|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} }} '''Kōtokui family''' (幸徳井家) refers to a {{ill|Jige-ke|ja|地下家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jige-ke|qq=|1=Jigeke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424052}} based in {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara|qq=}}, engaged in [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]] as their hereditary occupation. Though originally descendants of the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|qq=|1=Abe clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657247}}, the family became associated with the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}} after {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoyuki|ja|幸徳井友幸|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomoyuki|qq=|1=Kōtokui Tomoyuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q102247147}}, the founder, was adopted by the Kamo clan. {{see also|Kamo clan|Kadenokōji family (Kamo clan)}} == Overview == In 1391, Tomoyuki was born as the second son of {{ill|Abe no Tomouji|ja|安倍友氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Tomouji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a branch family of the Abe clan. Tomoyuki studied [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]] under {{ill|Kamo no Sadahiro|ja|賀茂定弘|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Sadahiro|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who served as {{ill|Gyōbu-kyō|ja|刑部卿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōbu-kyō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 刑部省|1=Ministry of Justice|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6867320}} and {{ill|Onmyōjō|ja|陰陽助|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōjō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 陰陽寮|1=Onmyō-ryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658260}}. In 1419, Tomoyuki was adopted by Sadahiro and took the name Kamo no Tomoyuki. He rose to the Rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}} by 1463 and established his residence in Kōtokui, a well-known area near {{ill|Takahata Village|ja|高畠村|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahata Village|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|1=Takahata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q772103}}, serving as a political advisor to {{ill|Jinson|ja|尋尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinson|qq=|1=Jinson|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7138674}} of {{ill|Daijōin Temple|ja|大乗院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōin Temple|qq=|1=Daijō-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16766994}}. The family name "Kōtokui" derives from this location. After Tomoyuki's death in 1473, his descendants continued to serve Daijōin Temple and played key roles in supervising [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]] and {{ill|Shōmonshi|ja|声聞師|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōmonshi|qq=}} in the Nara region, even compiling their own calendar, the {{ill|Kōtokui Calendar|ja|幸徳井暦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Calendar|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 南都暦}}. === Edo Period === In the early Edo period, {{ill|Tsuchimikado Yasuhiro|ja|土御門泰重|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Yasuhiro|qq=|1=Yasushige Tsuchimikado|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423489}} resigned from the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}} due to repeated failures, leaving a vacancy for {{ill|Onmyō no Kami|ja|陰陽頭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō no Kami|qq=|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}}. With the main Kamo family line extinct, {{ill|Kōtokui Tomokage|ja|幸徳井友景|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomokage|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the ninth head of the Kōtokui family, was appointed Onmyō no Kami in 1618. This appointment led to a series of Kōtokui family members, such as {{ill|Kōtokui Tomotane|ja|幸徳井友種|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomotane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoden|ja|幸徳井友傳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomoden|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, succeeding to this position. However, by the late 17th century, the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}} regained the title of Onmyō no Kami and stripped the Kōtokui family of their control over southern Nara's Onmyōji. Despite this, the Kōtokui family maintained influence over Nara temples and continued advising on temple rituals. === Meiji Period and Beyond === Following the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the Kōtokui family petitioned to be ennobled as [[:en:Kazoku|Kazoku]] due to their historical contributions. The petition was denied, and their activities after this period remain unknown. == Heads of the Kōtokui Family == 1. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoyuki|ja|幸徳井友幸|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomoyuki|qq=|1=Kōtokui Tomoyuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q102247147}} (1391–1473) - Senior Third Rank 2. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoshige|ja|幸徳井友重|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomoshige|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1411–1490) - Senior Third Rank 3. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomonobu|ja|幸徳井友延|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomonobu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1435–1494) - Junior Third Rank ... 24. {{ill|Kōtokui Yasuhiko|ja|幸徳井保章|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Yasuhiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1847–?) == References in Literature == * {{Cite book|author=Arayama Toru|title=Yagyū Onmyōken|year=2008|isbn=978-4-10-121042-1}} * {{ill|Azano Kōhei|ja|あざの耕平|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azano Kōhei|qq=|1=Kōhei Azano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11257270}}, ''Tokyo Ravens'' - A [[:en:Light novel|Light novel]] series featuring Onmyōji, including Kōtokui family members in a supporting role. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Chintaku Reifu Shrine|ja|鎮宅霊符神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chintaku Reifu Shrine|qq=}} * [[:en:Nara University of Education|Nara Educational University]] * {{ill|Kyobate Tenjinsha|ja|飛鳥神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyobate Tenjinsha|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|幸徳井家|version=100215448|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Kuge]] [[Category:Onmyōdō]] [[Category:History of Nara]] }} [[ja:幸徳井家]] [[Category:History of Nara]] [[Category:Kōtokuji Family]] [[Category:Soeage District]] [[Category:Kamo no Asomi Clan]] [[Category:Clans by region]] [[Category:Onmyōryō]] {{wikidata link|Q11483987}}

Though originally descendants of the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|1=Abe clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657247}}, the family became associated with the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}} after {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoyuki|ja|幸徳井友幸|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomoyuki|1=Kōtokui Tomoyuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q102247147}}, the founder, was adopted by the Kamo clan. {{see also|Kamo clan|Kadenokōji family (Kamo clan)}} == Overview == In 1391, Tomoyuki was born as the second son of {{ill|Abe no Tomouji|ja|安倍友氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Tomouji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a branch family of the Abe clan. Tomoyuki studied [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]] under {{ill|Kamo no Sadahiro|ja|賀茂定弘|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Sadahiro|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who served as {{ill|Gyōbu-kyō|ja|刑部卿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōbu-kyō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 刑部省|1=Ministry of Justice|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6867320}} and {{ill|Onmyōjō|ja|陰陽助|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōjō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 陰陽寮|1=Onmyō-ryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658260}}. In 1419, Tomoyuki was adopted by Sadahiro and took the name Kamo no Tomoyuki. He rose to the Rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}} by 1463 and established his residence in Kōtokui, a well-known area near {{ill|Takahata Village|ja|高畠村|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahata Village|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|1=Takahata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q772103}}, serving as a political advisor to {{ill|Jinson|ja|尋尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinson|1=Jinson|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7138674}} of {{ill|Daijōin Temple|ja|大乗院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōin Temple|1=Daijō-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16766994}}. The family name "Kōtokui" derives from this location. After Tomoyuki's death in 1473, his descendants continued to serve Daijōin Temple and played key roles in supervising [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]] and {{ill|Shōmonshi|ja|声聞師|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōmonshi|qq=}} in the Nara region, even compiling their own calendar, the {{ill|Kōtokui Calendar|ja|幸徳井暦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Calendar|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 南都暦}}. === Edo Period === In the early Edo period, {{ill|Tsuchimikado Yasuhiro|ja|土御門泰重|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Yasuhiro|1=Yasushige Tsuchimikado|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423489}} resigned from the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}} due to repeated failures, leaving a vacancy for {{ill|Onmyō no Kami|ja|陰陽頭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō no Kami|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}}. With the main Kamo family line extinct, {{ill|Kōtokui Tomokage|ja|幸徳井友景|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomokage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the ninth head of the Kōtokui family, was appointed Onmyō no Kami in 1618. This appointment led to a series of Kōtokui family members, such as {{ill|Kōtokui Tomotane|ja|幸徳井友種|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomotane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoden|ja|幸徳井友傳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomoden|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, succeeding to this position. However, by the late 17th century, the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}} regained the title of Onmyō no Kami and stripped the Kōtokui family of their control over southern Nara's Onmyōji. Despite this, the Kōtokui family maintained influence over Nara temples and continued advising on temple rituals. === Meiji Period and Beyond === Following the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the Kōtokui family petitioned to be ennobled as [[:en:Kazoku|Kazoku]] due to their historical contributions. The petition was denied, and their activities after this period remain unknown. == Heads of the Kōtokui Family == 1. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoyuki|ja|幸徳井友幸|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomoyuki|1=Kōtokui Tomoyuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q102247147}} (1391–1473) - Senior Third Rank 2. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoshige|ja|幸徳井友重|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomoshige|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1411–1490) - Senior Third Rank 3. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomonobu|ja|幸徳井友延|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomonobu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1435–1494) - Junior Third Rank ... 24. {{ill|Kōtokui Yasuhiko|ja|幸徳井保章|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Yasuhiko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1847–?) == References in Literature == * {{Cite book|author=Arayama Toru|title=Yagyū Onmyōken|year=2008|isbn=978-4-10-121042-1}} * {{ill|Azano Kōhei|ja|あざの耕平|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azano Kōhei|1=Kōhei Azano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11257270}}, ''Tokyo Ravens'' - A [[:en:Light novel|Light novel]] series featuring Onmyōji, including Kōtokui family members in a supporting role. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Chintaku Reifu Shrine|ja|鎮宅霊符神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chintaku Reifu Shrine|qq=}} * [[:en:Nara University of Education|Nara Educational University]] * {{ill|Kyobate Tenjinsha|ja|飛鳥神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyobate Tenjinsha|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|幸徳井家|version=100215448|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Kuge]] [[Category:Onmyōdō]] [[Category:History of Nara]] }} [[ja:幸徳井家]] [[Category:History of Nara]] [[Category:Kōtokuji Family]] [[Category:Soeage District]] [[Category:Kamo no Asomi Clan]] [[Category:Clans by region]] [[Category:Onmyōryō]] {{wikidata link|Q11483987}}

However, by the late 17th century, the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}} regained the title of Onmyō no Kami and stripped the Kōtokui family of their control over southern Nara's Onmyōji. Despite this, the Kōtokui family maintained influence over Nara temples and continued advising on temple rituals. === Meiji Period and Beyond === Following the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the Kōtokui family petitioned to be ennobled as [[:en:Kazoku|Kazoku]] due to their historical contributions. The petition was denied, and their activities after this period remain unknown. == Heads of the Kōtokui Family == 1. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoyuki|ja|幸徳井友幸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomoyuki|1=Kōtokui Tomoyuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q102247147}} (1391–1473) - Senior Third Rank 2. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoshige|ja|幸徳井友重|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomoshige|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1411–1490) - Senior Third Rank 3. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomonobu|ja|幸徳井友延|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomonobu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1435–1494) - Junior Third Rank ... 24. {{ill|Kōtokui Yasuhiko|ja|幸徳井保章|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Yasuhiko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1847–?) == References in Literature == * {{Cite book|author=Arayama Toru|title=Yagyū Onmyōken|year=2008|isbn=978-4-10-121042-1}} * {{ill|Azano Kōhei|ja|あざの耕平|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azano Kōhei|1=Kōhei Azano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11257270}}, ''Tokyo Ravens'' - A [[:en:Light novel|Light novel]] series featuring Onmyōji, including Kōtokui family members in a supporting role. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Chintaku Reifu Shrine|ja|鎮宅霊符神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chintaku Reifu Shrine|qq=}} * [[:en:Nara University of Education|Nara Educational University]] * {{ill|Kyobate Tenjinsha|ja|飛鳥神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyobate Tenjinsha|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|幸徳井家|version=100215448|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Kuge]] [[Category:Onmyōdō]] [[Category:History of Nara]] }} [[ja:幸徳井家]] [[Category:History of Nara]] [[Category:Kōtokuji Family]] [[Category:Soeage District]] [[Category:Kamo no Asomi Clan]] [[Category:Clans by region]] [[Category:Onmyōryō]] {{wikidata link|Q11483987}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

[[ja:幸徳井家]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11483987]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:50, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Refimprove|date=October 2019}} {{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Kōtokui family | crest = Unknown.jpg | crest_caption = | main_house = {{ill|Kamo no Asomi clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Asomi clan|1=Kamo no Ason clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q122979457}} (branch family) | founder = {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoyuki|ja|幸徳井友幸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomoyuki|1=Kōtokui Tomoyuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q102247147}} | type = {{ill|Jige-ke|ja|地下家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jige-ke|1=Jigeke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424052}}
[[:en:Shizoku|Shizoku]] | origin = [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] Kōtokui in Takahata Village | residence = Kōtokui, Takahata Village, Yamato Province
Present-day [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]] Takahatachō and Onyōchō | notable_members = {{ill|Kōtokui Tomokage|ja|幸徳井友景|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomokage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} }} '''Kōtokui family''' (幸徳井家) refers to a {{ill|Jige-ke|ja|地下家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jige-ke|1=Jigeke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424052}} based in {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara|qq=}}, engaged in [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]] as their hereditary occupation. Though originally descendants of the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|1=Abe clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657247}}, the family became associated with the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}} after {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoyuki|ja|幸徳井友幸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomoyuki|1=Kōtokui Tomoyuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q102247147}}, the founder, was adopted by the Kamo clan. {{see also|Kamo clan|Kadenokōji family (Kamo clan)}} == Overview == In 1391, Tomoyuki was born as the second son of {{ill|Abe no Tomouji|ja|安倍友氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Tomouji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a branch family of the Abe clan. Tomoyuki studied [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]] under {{ill|Kamo no Sadahiro|ja|賀茂定弘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Sadahiro|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who served as {{ill|Gyōbu-kyō|ja|刑部卿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōbu-kyō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 刑部省|1=Ministry of Justice|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6867320}} and {{ill|Onmyōjō|ja|陰陽助|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōjō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 陰陽寮|1=Onmyō-ryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658260}}. In 1419, Tomoyuki was adopted by Sadahiro and took the name Kamo no Tomoyuki. He rose to the Rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}} by 1463 and established his residence in Kōtokui, a well-known area near {{ill|Takahata Village|ja|高畠村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahata Village|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|1=Takahata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q772103}}, serving as a political advisor to {{ill|Jinson|ja|尋尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinson|1=Jinson|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7138674}} of {{ill|Daijōin Temple|ja|大乗院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōin Temple|1=Daijō-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16766994}}. The family name "Kōtokui" derives from this location. After Tomoyuki's death in 1473, his descendants continued to serve Daijōin Temple and played key roles in supervising [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]] and {{ill|Shōmonshi|ja|声聞師|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōmonshi|qq=}} in the Nara region, even compiling their own calendar, the {{ill|Kōtokui Calendar|ja|幸徳井暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Calendar|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 南都暦}}. === Edo Period === In the early Edo period, {{ill|Tsuchimikado Yasuhiro|ja|土御門泰重|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Yasuhiro|1=Yasushige Tsuchimikado|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423489}} resigned from the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}} due to repeated failures, leaving a vacancy for {{ill|Onmyō no Kami|ja|陰陽頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō no Kami|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}}. With the main Kamo family line extinct, {{ill|Kōtokui Tomokage|ja|幸徳井友景|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomokage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the ninth head of the Kōtokui family, was appointed Onmyō no Kami in 1618. This appointment led to a series of Kōtokui family members, such as {{ill|Kōtokui Tomotane|ja|幸徳井友種|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomotane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoden|ja|幸徳井友傳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomoden|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, succeeding to this position. However, by the late 17th century, the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}} regained the title of Onmyō no Kami and stripped the Kōtokui family of their control over southern Nara's Onmyōji. Despite this, the Kōtokui family maintained influence over Nara temples and continued advising on temple rituals. === Meiji Period and Beyond === Following the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the Kōtokui family petitioned to be ennobled as [[:en:Kazoku|Kazoku]] due to their historical contributions. The petition was denied, and their activities after this period remain unknown. == Heads of the Kōtokui Family == 1. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoyuki|ja|幸徳井友幸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomoyuki|1=Kōtokui Tomoyuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q102247147}} (1391–1473) - Senior Third Rank 2. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoshige|ja|幸徳井友重|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomoshige|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1411–1490) - Senior Third Rank 3. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomonobu|ja|幸徳井友延|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomonobu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1435–1494) - Junior Third Rank ... 24. {{ill|Kōtokui Yasuhiko|ja|幸徳井保章|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Yasuhiko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1847–?) == References in Literature == * {{Cite book|author=Arayama Toru|title=Yagyū Onmyōken|year=2008|isbn=978-4-10-121042-1}} * {{ill|Azano Kōhei|ja|あざの耕平|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azano Kōhei|1=Kōhei Azano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11257270}}, ''Tokyo Ravens'' - A [[:en:Light novel|Light novel]] series featuring Onmyōji, including Kōtokui family members in a supporting role. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Chintaku Reifu Shrine|ja|鎮宅霊符神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chintaku Reifu Shrine|qq=}} * [[:en:Nara University of Education|Nara Educational University]] * {{ill|Kyobate Tenjinsha|ja|飛鳥神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyobate Tenjinsha|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|幸徳井家|version=100215448|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Kuge]] [[Category:Onmyōdō]] [[Category:History of Nara]] }} [[ja:幸徳井家]] [[Category:History of Nara]] [[Category:Kōtokuji Family]] [[Category:Soeage District]] [[Category:Kamo no Asomi Clan]] [[Category:Clans by region]] [[Category:Onmyōryō]] {{wikidata link|Q11483987}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

Tomoyuki studied [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]] under {{ill|Kamo no Sadahiro|ja|賀茂定弘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Sadahiro|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who served as {{ill|Gyōbu-kyō|ja|刑部卿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōbu-kyō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 刑部省|1=Ministry of Justice|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6867320}} and {{ill|Onmyōjō|ja|陰陽助|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōjō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 陰陽寮|1=Onmyō-ryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658260}}. In 1419, Tomoyuki was adopted by Sadahiro and took the name Kamo no Tomoyuki. He rose to the Rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}} by 1463 and established his residence in Kōtokui, a well-known area near {{ill|Takahata Village|ja|高畠村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahata Village|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|1=Takahata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q772103}}, serving as a political advisor to {{ill|Jinson|ja|尋尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinson|1=Jinson|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7138674}} of {{ill|Daijōin Temple|ja|大乗院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōin Temple|1=Daijō-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16766994}}. The family name "Kōtokui" derives from this location. After Tomoyuki's death in 1473, his descendants continued to serve Daijōin Temple and played key roles in supervising [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]] and {{ill|Shōmonshi|ja|声聞師|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōmonshi|qq=}} in the Nara region, even compiling their own calendar, the {{ill|Kōtokui Calendar|ja|幸徳井暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Calendar|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 南都暦}}. === Edo Period === In the early Edo period, {{ill|Tsuchimikado Yasuhiro|ja|土御門泰重|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Yasuhiro|1=Yasushige Tsuchimikado|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423489}} resigned from the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}} due to repeated failures, leaving a vacancy for {{ill|Onmyō no Kami|ja|陰陽頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō no Kami|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}}. With the main Kamo family line extinct, {{ill|Kōtokui Tomokage|ja|幸徳井友景|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomokage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the ninth head of the Kōtokui family, was appointed Onmyō no Kami in 1618. This appointment led to a series of Kōtokui family members, such as {{ill|Kōtokui Tomotane|ja|幸徳井友種|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomotane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoden|ja|幸徳井友傳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomoden|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, succeeding to this position. However, by the late 17th century, the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}} regained the title of Onmyō no Kami and stripped the Kōtokui family of their control over southern Nara's Onmyōji. Despite this, the Kōtokui family maintained influence over Nara temples and continued advising on temple rituals. === Meiji Period and Beyond === Following the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the Kōtokui family petitioned to be ennobled as [[:en:Kazoku|Kazoku]] due to their historical contributions. The petition was denied, and their activities after this period remain unknown. == Heads of the Kōtokui Family == 1. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoyuki|ja|幸徳井友幸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomoyuki|1=Kōtokui Tomoyuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q102247147}} (1391–1473) - Senior Third Rank 2. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoshige|ja|幸徳井友重|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomoshige|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1411–1490) - Senior Third Rank 3. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomonobu|ja|幸徳井友延|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomonobu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1435–1494) - Junior Third Rank ... 24. {{ill|Kōtokui Yasuhiko|ja|幸徳井保章|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Yasuhiko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1847–?) == References in Literature == * {{Cite book|author=Arayama Toru|title=Yagyū Onmyōken|year=2008|isbn=978-4-10-121042-1}} * {{ill|Azano Kōhei|ja|あざの耕平|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azano Kōhei|1=Kōhei Azano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11257270}}, ''Tokyo Ravens'' - A [[:en:Light novel|Light novel]] series featuring Onmyōji, including Kōtokui family members in a supporting role. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Chintaku Reifu Shrine|ja|鎮宅霊符神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chintaku Reifu Shrine|qq=}} * [[:en:Nara University of Education|Nara Educational University]] * {{ill|Kyobate Tenjinsha|ja|飛鳥神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyobate Tenjinsha|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|幸徳井家|version=100215448|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Kuge]] [[Category:Onmyōdō]] [[Category:History of Nara]] }} [[ja:幸徳井家]] [[Category:History of Nara]] [[Category:Kōtokuji Family]] [[Category:Soeage District]] [[Category:Kamo no Asomi Clan]] [[Category:Clans by region]] [[Category:Onmyōryō]] {{wikidata link|Q11483987}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

A [[:en:Light novel|Light novel]] series featuring Onmyōji, including Kōtokui family members in a supporting role. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Chintaku Reifu Shrine|ja|鎮宅霊符神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chintaku Reifu Shrine|qq=}} * [[:en:Nara University of Education|Nara Educational University]] * {{ill|Kyobate Tenjinsha|ja|飛鳥神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyobate Tenjinsha|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|幸徳井家|version=100215448|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Kuge]] [[Category:Onmyōdō]] [[Category:History of Nara]] }} [[ja:幸徳井家]] [[Category:History of Nara]] [[Category:Kōtokuji Family]] [[Category:Soeage District]] [[Category:Kamo no Asomi Clan]] [[Category:Clans by region]] [[Category:Onmyōryō]] {{wikidata link|Q11483987}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[ja:幸徳井家]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11483987]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:50, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Refimprove|date=October 2019}} {{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Kōtokui family | crest = Unknown.jpg | crest_caption = | main_house = {{ill|Kamo no Asomi clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Asomi clan|1=Kamo no Ason clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q122979457}} (branch family) | founder = {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoyuki|ja|幸徳井友幸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomoyuki|1=Kōtokui Tomoyuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q102247147}} | type = {{ill|Jige-ke|ja|地下家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jige-ke|1=Jigeke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424052}}
[[:en:Shizoku|Shizoku]] | origin = [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] Kōtokui in Takahata Village | residence = Kōtokui, Takahata Village, Yamato Province
Present-day [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]] Takahatachō and Onyōchō | notable_members = {{ill|Kōtokui Tomokage|ja|幸徳井友景|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomokage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} }} '''Kōtokui family''' (幸徳井家) refers to a {{ill|Jige-ke|ja|地下家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jige-ke|1=Jigeke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424052}} based in {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara|qq=}}, engaged in [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]] as their hereditary occupation. Though originally descendants of the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|1=Abe clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657247}}, the family became associated with the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}} after {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoyuki|ja|幸徳井友幸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomoyuki|1=Kōtokui Tomoyuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q102247147}}, the founder, was adopted by the Kamo clan. {{see also|Kamo clan|Kadenokōji family (Kamo clan)}} == Overview == In 1391, Tomoyuki was born as the second son of {{ill|Abe no Tomouji|ja|安倍友氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Tomouji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a branch family of the Abe clan. Tomoyuki studied [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]] under {{ill|Kamo no Sadahiro|ja|賀茂定弘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Sadahiro|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who served as {{ill|Gyōbu-kyō|ja|刑部卿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōbu-kyō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 刑部省|1=Ministry of Justice|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6867320}} and {{ill|Onmyōjō|ja|陰陽助|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōjō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 陰陽寮|1=Onmyō-ryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658260}}. In 1419, Tomoyuki was adopted by Sadahiro and took the name Kamo no Tomoyuki. He rose to the Rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}} by 1463 and established his residence in Kōtokui, a well-known area near {{ill|Takahata Village|ja|高畠村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahata Village|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|1=Takahata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q772103}}, serving as a political advisor to {{ill|Jinson|ja|尋尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinson|1=Jinson|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7138674}} of {{ill|Daijōin Temple|ja|大乗院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōin Temple|1=Daijō-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16766994}}. The family name "Kōtokui" derives from this location. After Tomoyuki's death in 1473, his descendants continued to serve Daijōin Temple and played key roles in supervising [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]] and {{ill|Shōmonshi|ja|声聞師|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōmonshi|qq=}} in the Nara region, even compiling their own calendar, the {{ill|Kōtokui Calendar|ja|幸徳井暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Calendar|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 南都暦}}. === Edo Period === In the early Edo period, {{ill|Tsuchimikado Yasuhiro|ja|土御門泰重|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Yasuhiro|1=Yasushige Tsuchimikado|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423489}} resigned from the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}} due to repeated failures, leaving a vacancy for {{ill|Onmyō no Kami|ja|陰陽頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō no Kami|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}}. With the main Kamo family line extinct, {{ill|Kōtokui Tomokage|ja|幸徳井友景|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomokage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the ninth head of the Kōtokui family, was appointed Onmyō no Kami in 1618. This appointment led to a series of Kōtokui family members, such as {{ill|Kōtokui Tomotane|ja|幸徳井友種|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomotane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoden|ja|幸徳井友傳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomoden|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, succeeding to this position. However, by the late 17th century, the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}} regained the title of Onmyō no Kami and stripped the Kōtokui family of their control over southern Nara's Onmyōji. Despite this, the Kōtokui family maintained influence over Nara temples and continued advising on temple rituals. === Meiji Period and Beyond === Following the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the Kōtokui family petitioned to be ennobled as [[:en:Kazoku|Kazoku]] due to their historical contributions. The petition was denied, and their activities after this period remain unknown. == Heads of the Kōtokui Family == 1. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoyuki|ja|幸徳井友幸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomoyuki|1=Kōtokui Tomoyuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q102247147}} (1391–1473) - Senior Third Rank 2. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoshige|ja|幸徳井友重|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomoshige|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1411–1490) - Senior Third Rank 3. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomonobu|ja|幸徳井友延|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomonobu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1435–1494) - Junior Third Rank ... 24. {{ill|Kōtokui Yasuhiko|ja|幸徳井保章|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Yasuhiko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1847–?) == References in Literature == * {{Cite book|author=Arayama Toru|title=Yagyū Onmyōken|year=2008|isbn=978-4-10-121042-1}} * {{ill|Azano Kōhei|ja|あざの耕平|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azano Kōhei|1=Kōhei Azano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11257270}}, ''Tokyo Ravens'' - A [[:en:Light novel|Light novel]] series featuring Onmyōji, including Kōtokui family members in a supporting role. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Chintaku Reifu Shrine|ja|鎮宅霊符神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chintaku Reifu Shrine|qq=}} * [[:en:Nara University of Education|Nara Educational University]] * {{ill|Kyobate Tenjinsha|ja|飛鳥神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyobate Tenjinsha|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|幸徳井家|version=100215448|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Kuge]] [[Category:Onmyōdō]] [[Category:History of Nara]] }} [[ja:幸徳井家]] [[Category:History of Nara]] [[Category:Kōtokuji Family]] [[Category:Soeage District]] [[Category:Kamo no Asomi Clan]] [[Category:Clans by region]] [[Category:Onmyōryō]] {{wikidata link|Q11483987}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

The petition was denied, and their activities after this period remain unknown. == Heads of the Kōtokui Family == 1. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoyuki|ja|幸徳井友幸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomoyuki|1=Kōtokui Tomoyuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q102247147}} (1391–1473) - Senior Third Rank 2. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoshige|ja|幸徳井友重|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomoshige|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1411–1490) - Senior Third Rank 3. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomonobu|ja|幸徳井友延|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomonobu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1435–1494) - Junior Third Rank ... 24. {{ill|Kōtokui Yasuhiko|ja|幸徳井保章|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Yasuhiko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1847–?) == References in Literature == * {{Cite book|author=Arayama Toru|title=Yagyū Onmyōken|year=2008|isbn=978-4-10-121042-1}} * {{ill|Azano Kōhei|ja|あざの耕平|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azano Kōhei|1=Kōhei Azano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11257270}}, ''Tokyo Ravens'' - A [[:en:Light novel|Light novel]] series featuring Onmyōji, including Kōtokui family members in a supporting role. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Chintaku Reifu Shrine|ja|鎮宅霊符神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chintaku Reifu Shrine|qq=}} * [[:en:Nara University of Education|Nara Educational University]] * {{ill|Kyobate Tenjinsha|ja|飛鳥神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyobate Tenjinsha|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|幸徳井家|version=100215448|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Kuge]] [[Category:Onmyōdō]] [[Category:History of Nara]] }} [[ja:幸徳井家]] [[Category:History of Nara]] [[Category:Kōtokuji Family]] [[Category:Soeage District]] [[Category:Kamo no Asomi Clan]] [[Category:Clans by region]] [[Category:Onmyōryō]] {{wikidata link|Q11483987}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

Tomoyuki studied [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]] under {{ill|Kamo no Sadahiro|ja|賀茂定弘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Sadahiro|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who served as {{ill|Gyōbu-kyō|ja|刑部卿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōbu-kyō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 刑部省|1=Ministry of Justice|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6867320}} and {{ill|Onmyōjō|ja|陰陽助|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōjō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 陰陽寮|1=Onmyō-ryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658260}}. In 1419, Tomoyuki was adopted by Sadahiro and took the name Kamo no Tomoyuki. He rose to the Rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}} by 1463 and established his residence in Kōtokui, a well-known area near {{ill|Takahata Village|ja|高畠村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahata Village|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|1=Takahata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q772103}}, serving as a political advisor to {{ill|Jinson|ja|尋尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinson|1=Jinson|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7138674}} of {{ill|Daijōin Temple|ja|大乗院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōin Temple|1=Daijō-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16766994}}. The family name "Kōtokui" derives from this location. After Tomoyuki's death in 1473, his descendants continued to serve Daijōin Temple and played key roles in supervising [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]] and {{ill|Shōmonshi|ja|声聞師|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōmonshi|qq=}} in the Nara region, even compiling their own calendar, the {{ill|Kōtokui Calendar|ja|幸徳井暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Calendar|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 南都暦}}. === Edo Period === In the early Edo period, {{ill|Tsuchimikado Yasuhiro|ja|土御門泰重|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Yasuhiro|1=Yasushige Tsuchimikado|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423489}} resigned from the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}} due to repeated failures, leaving a vacancy for {{ill|Onmyō no Kami|ja|陰陽頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō no Kami|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}}. With the main Kamo family line extinct, {{ill|Kōtokui Tomokage|ja|幸徳井友景|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomokage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the ninth head of the Kōtokui family, was appointed Onmyō no Kami in 1618. This appointment led to a series of Kōtokui family members, such as {{ill|Kōtokui Tomotane|ja|幸徳井友種|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomotane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoden|ja|幸徳井友傳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomoden|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, succeeding to this position. However, by the late 17th century, the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}} regained the title of Onmyō no Kami and stripped the Kōtokui family of their control over southern Nara's Onmyōji. Despite this, the Kōtokui family maintained influence over Nara temples and continued advising on temple rituals. === Meiji Period and Beyond === Following the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the Kōtokui family petitioned to be ennobled as [[:en:Kazoku|Kazoku]] due to their historical contributions. The petition was denied, and their activities after this period remain unknown. == Heads of the Kōtokui Family == 1. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoyuki|ja|幸徳井友幸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomoyuki|1=Kōtokui Tomoyuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q102247147}} (1391–1473) - Senior Third Rank 2. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoshige|ja|幸徳井友重|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomoshige|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1411–1490) - Senior Third Rank 3. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomonobu|ja|幸徳井友延|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomonobu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1435–1494) - Junior Third Rank ... 24. {{ill|Kōtokui Yasuhiko|ja|幸徳井保章|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Yasuhiko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1847–?) == References in Literature == * {{Cite book|author=Arayama Toru|title=Yagyū Onmyōken|year=2008|isbn=978-4-10-121042-1}} * {{ill|Azano Kōhei|ja|あざの耕平|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azano Kōhei|1=Kōhei Azano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11257270}}, ''Tokyo Ravens'' - A [[:en:Light novel|Light novel]] series featuring Onmyōji, including Kōtokui family members in a supporting role. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Chintaku Reifu Shrine|ja|鎮宅霊符神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chintaku Reifu Shrine|qq=}} * [[:en:Nara University of Education|Nara Educational University]] * {{ill|Kyobate Tenjinsha|ja|飛鳥神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyobate Tenjinsha|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|幸徳井家|version=100215448|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Kuge]] [[Category:Onmyōdō]] [[Category:History of Nara]] }} [[ja:幸徳井家]] [[Category:History of Nara]] [[Category:Kōtokuji Family]] [[Category:Soeage District]] [[Category:Kamo no Asomi Clan]] [[Category:Clans by region]] [[Category:Onmyōryō]] {{wikidata link|Q11483987}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

Tomoyuki studied [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]] under {{ill|Kamo no Sadahiro|ja|賀茂定弘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Sadahiro|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who served as {{ill|Gyōbu-kyō|ja|刑部卿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōbu-kyō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 刑部省|1=Ministry of Justice|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6867320}} and {{ill|Onmyōjō|ja|陰陽助|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōjō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 陰陽寮|1=Onmyō-ryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658260}}. In 1419, Tomoyuki was adopted by Sadahiro and took the name Kamo no Tomoyuki. He rose to the Rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}} by 1463 and established his residence in Kōtokui, a well-known area near {{ill|Takahata Village|ja|高畠村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahata Village|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|1=Takahata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q772103}}, serving as a political advisor to {{ill|Jinson|ja|尋尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinson|1=Jinson|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7138674}} of {{ill|Daijōin Temple|ja|大乗院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōin Temple|1=Daijō-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16766994}}. With the main Kamo family line extinct, {{ill|Kōtokui Tomokage|ja|幸徳井友景|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomokage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the ninth head of the Kōtokui family, was appointed Onmyō no Kami in 1618. This appointment led to a series of Kōtokui family members, such as {{ill|Kōtokui Tomotane|ja|幸徳井友種|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomotane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoden|ja|幸徳井友傳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomoden|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, succeeding to this position. Despite this, the Kōtokui family maintained influence over Nara temples and continued advising on temple rituals. === Meiji Period and Beyond === Following the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the Kōtokui family petitioned to be ennobled as [[:en:Kazoku|Kazoku]] due to their historical contributions. The petition was denied, and their activities after this period remain unknown. == Heads of the Kōtokui Family == 1. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoyuki|ja|幸徳井友幸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomoyuki|1=Kōtokui Tomoyuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q102247147}} (1391–1473) - Senior Third Rank 2. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoshige|ja|幸徳井友重|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomoshige|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1411–1490) - Senior Third Rank 3. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomonobu|ja|幸徳井友延|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomonobu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1435–1494) - Junior Third Rank ... 24. {{ill|Kōtokui Yasuhiko|ja|幸徳井保章|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Yasuhiko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1847–?) == References in Literature == * {{Cite book|author=Arayama Toru|title=Yagyū Onmyōken|year=2008|isbn=978-4-10-121042-1}} * {{ill|Azano Kōhei|ja|あざの耕平|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azano Kōhei|1=Kōhei Azano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11257270}}, ''Tokyo Ravens'' - A [[:en:Light novel|Light novel]] series featuring Onmyōji, including Kōtokui family members in a supporting role. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Chintaku Reifu Shrine|ja|鎮宅霊符神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chintaku Reifu Shrine|qq=}} * [[:en:Nara University of Education|Nara Educational University]] * {{ill|Kyobate Tenjinsha|ja|飛鳥神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyobate Tenjinsha|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|幸徳井家|version=100215448|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:幸徳井家]] {{wikidata link|Q11483987}} [[Category:Clans by region]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:History of Nara]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Kamo no Asomi Clan]] [[Category:Kuge]] [[Category:Kōtokuji Family]] [[Category:Onmyōdō]] [[Category:Onmyōryō]] [[Category:Soeage District]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

The petition was denied, and their activities after this period remain unknown. == Heads of the Kōtokui Family == 1. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoyuki|ja|幸徳井友幸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomoyuki|1=Kōtokui Tomoyuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q102247147}} (1391–1473) - Senior Third Rank 2. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoshige|ja|幸徳井友重|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomoshige|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1411–1490) - Senior Third Rank 3. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomonobu|ja|幸徳井友延|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomonobu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1435–1494) - Junior Third Rank ... 24. {{ill|Kōtokui Yasuhiko|ja|幸徳井保章|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Yasuhiko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1847–?) == References in Literature == * {{Cite book|author=Arayama Toru|title=Yagyū Onmyōken|year=2008|isbn=978-4-10-121042-1}} * {{ill|Azano Kōhei|ja|あざの耕平|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azano Kōhei|1=Kōhei Azano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11257270}}, ''Tokyo Ravens'' - A [[:en:Light novel|Light novel]] series featuring Onmyōji, including Kōtokui family members in a supporting role. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Chintaku Reifu Shrine|ja|鎮宅霊符神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chintaku Reifu Shrine|qq=}} * [[:en:Nara University of Education|Nara Educational University]] * {{ill|Kyobate Tenjinsha|ja|飛鳥神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyobate Tenjinsha|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|幸徳井家|version=100215448|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:幸徳井家]] {{wikidata link|Q11483987}} [[Category:Clans by region]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:History of Nara]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Kamo no Asomi Clan]] [[Category:Kuge]] [[Category:Kōtokuji Family]] [[Category:Onmyōdō]] [[Category:Onmyōryō]] [[Category:Soeage District]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

[[ja:幸徳井家]] [[ja:幸徳井家]] [[ja:幸徳井家]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11483987]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:50, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Refimprove|date=October 2019}} {{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Kōtokui family | crest = Unknown.jpg | crest_caption = | main_house = {{ill|Kamo no Asomi clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Asomi clan|1=Kamo no Ason clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q122979457}} (branch family) | founder = {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoyuki|ja|幸徳井友幸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomoyuki|1=Kōtokui Tomoyuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q102247147}} | type = {{ill|Jige-ke|ja|地下家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jige-ke|1=Jigeke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424052}}
[[:en:Shizoku|Shizoku]] | origin = [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] Kōtokui in Takahata Village | residence = Kōtokui, Takahata Village, Yamato Province
Present-day [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]] Takahatachō and Onyōchō | notable_members = {{ill|Kōtokui Tomokage|ja|幸徳井友景|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomokage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} }} '''Kōtokui family''' (幸徳井家) refers to a {{ill|Jige-ke|ja|地下家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jige-ke|1=Jigeke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424052}} based in {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara|qq=}}, engaged in [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]] as their hereditary occupation. Though originally descendants of the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|1=Abe clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657247}}, the family became associated with the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}} after {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoyuki|ja|幸徳井友幸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomoyuki|1=Kōtokui Tomoyuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q102247147}}, the founder, was adopted by the Kamo clan. {{see also|Kamo clan|Kadenokōji family (Kamo clan)}} == Overview == In 1391, Tomoyuki was born as the second son of {{ill|Abe no Tomouji|ja|安倍友氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Tomouji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a branch family of the Abe clan. Tomoyuki studied [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]] under {{ill|Kamo no Sadahiro|ja|賀茂定弘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Sadahiro|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who served as {{ill|Gyōbu-kyō|ja|刑部卿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōbu-kyō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 刑部省|1=Ministry of Justice|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6867320}} and {{ill|Onmyōjō|ja|陰陽助|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōjō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 陰陽寮|1=Onmyō-ryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658260}}. In 1419, Tomoyuki was adopted by Sadahiro and took the name Kamo no Tomoyuki. He rose to the Rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}} by 1463 and established his residence in Kōtokui, a well-known area near {{ill|Takahata Village|ja|高畠村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahata Village|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|1=Takahata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q772103}}, serving as a political advisor to {{ill|Jinson|ja|尋尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinson|1=Jinson|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7138674}} of {{ill|Daijōin Temple|ja|大乗院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōin Temple|1=Daijō-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16766994}}. The family name "Kōtokui" derives from this location. After Tomoyuki's death in 1473, his descendants continued to serve Daijōin Temple and played key roles in supervising [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]] and {{ill|Shōmonshi|ja|声聞師|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōmonshi|qq=}} in the Nara region, even compiling their own calendar, the {{ill|Kōtokui Calendar|ja|幸徳井暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Calendar|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 南都暦}}. === Edo Period === In the early Edo period, {{ill|Tsuchimikado Yasuhiro|ja|土御門泰重|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Yasuhiro|1=Yasushige Tsuchimikado|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423489}} resigned from the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}} due to repeated failures, leaving a vacancy for {{ill|Onmyō no Kami|ja|陰陽頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō no Kami|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}}. With the main Kamo family line extinct, {{ill|Kōtokui Tomokage|ja|幸徳井友景|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomokage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the ninth head of the Kōtokui family, was appointed Onmyō no Kami in 1618. This appointment led to a series of Kōtokui family members, such as {{ill|Kōtokui Tomotane|ja|幸徳井友種|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomotane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoden|ja|幸徳井友傳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomoden|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, succeeding to this position. However, by the late 17th century, the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}} regained the title of Onmyō no Kami and stripped the Kōtokui family of their control over southern Nara's Onmyōji. Despite this, the Kōtokui family maintained influence over Nara temples and continued advising on temple rituals. === Meiji Period and Beyond === Following the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the Kōtokui family petitioned to be ennobled as [[:en:Kazoku|Kazoku]] due to their historical contributions. The petition was denied, and their activities after this period remain unknown. == Heads of the Kōtokui Family == 1. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoyuki|ja|幸徳井友幸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomoyuki|1=Kōtokui Tomoyuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q102247147}} (1391–1473) - Senior Third Rank 2. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoshige|ja|幸徳井友重|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomoshige|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1411–1490) - Senior Third Rank 3. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomonobu|ja|幸徳井友延|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomonobu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1435–1494) - Junior Third Rank ... 24. {{ill|Kōtokui Yasuhiko|ja|幸徳井保章|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Yasuhiko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1847–?) == References in Literature == * {{Cite book|author=Arayama Toru|title=Yagyū Onmyōken|year=2008|isbn=978-4-10-121042-1}} * {{ill|Azano Kōhei|ja|あざの耕平|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azano Kōhei|1=Kōhei Azano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11257270}}, ''Tokyo Ravens'' - A [[:en:Light novel|Light novel]] series featuring Onmyōji, including Kōtokui family members in a supporting role. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Chintaku Reifu Shrine|ja|鎮宅霊符神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chintaku Reifu Shrine|qq=}} * [[:en:Nara University of Education|Nara Educational University]] * {{ill|Kyobate Tenjinsha|ja|飛鳥神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyobate Tenjinsha|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|幸徳井家|version=100215448|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:幸徳井家]] {{wikidata link|Q11483987}} [[Category:Clans by region]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:History of Nara]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Kamo no Asomi Clan]] [[Category:Kuge]] [[Category:Kōtokuji Family]] [[Category:Onmyōdō]] [[Category:Onmyōryō]] [[Category:Soeage District]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

Tomoyuki studied [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]] under {{ill|Kamo no Sadahiro|ja|賀茂定弘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Sadahiro|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who served as {{ill|Gyōbu-kyō|ja|刑部卿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōbu-kyō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 刑部省|1=Ministry of Justice|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6867320}} and {{ill|Onmyōjō|ja|陰陽助|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōjō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 陰陽寮|1=Onmyō-ryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658260}}. In 1419, Tomoyuki was adopted by Sadahiro and took the name Kamo no Tomoyuki. He rose to the Rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}} by 1463 and established his residence in Kōtokui, a well-known area near {{ill|Takahata Village|ja|高畠村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahata Village|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|1=Takahata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q772103}}, serving as a political advisor to {{ill|Jinson|ja|尋尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinson|1=Jinson|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7138674}} of {{ill|Daijōin Temple|ja|大乗院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōin Temple|1=Daijō-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16766994}}. The family name "Kōtokui" derives from this location. After Tomoyuki's death in 1473, his descendants continued to serve Daijōin Temple and played key roles in supervising [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]] and {{ill|Shōmonshi|ja|声聞師|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōmonshi|qq=}} in the Nara region, even compiling their own calendar, the {{ill|Kōtokui Calendar|ja|幸徳井暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Calendar|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 南都暦}}. === Edo Period === In the early Edo period, {{ill|Tsuchimikado Yasuhiro|ja|土御門泰重|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Yasuhiro|1=Yasushige Tsuchimikado|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423489}} resigned from the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}} due to repeated failures, leaving a vacancy for {{ill|Onmyō no Kami|ja|陰陽頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō no Kami|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}}. With the main Kamo family line extinct, {{ill|Kōtokui Tomokage|ja|幸徳井友景|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomokage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the ninth head of the Kōtokui family, was appointed Onmyō no Kami in 1618. This appointment led to a series of Kōtokui family members, such as {{ill|Kōtokui Tomotane|ja|幸徳井友種|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomotane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoden|ja|幸徳井友傳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomoden|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, succeeding to this position. However, by the late 17th century, the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}} regained the title of Onmyō no Kami and stripped the Kōtokui family of their control over southern Nara's Onmyōji. Despite this, the Kōtokui family maintained influence over Nara temples and continued advising on temple rituals. === Meiji Period and Beyond === Following the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the Kōtokui family petitioned to be ennobled as [[:en:Kazoku|Kazoku]] due to their historical contributions. The petition was denied, and their activities after this period remain unknown. == Heads of the Kōtokui Family == 1. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoyuki|ja|幸徳井友幸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomoyuki|1=Kōtokui Tomoyuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q102247147}} (1391–1473) - Senior Third Rank 2. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomoshige|ja|幸徳井友重|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomoshige|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1411–1490) - Senior Third Rank 3. {{ill|Kōtokui Tomonobu|ja|幸徳井友延|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Tomonobu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1435–1494) - Junior Third Rank ... 24. {{ill|Kōtokui Yasuhiko|ja|幸徳井保章|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtokui Yasuhiko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1847–?) == References in Literature == * {{Cite book|author=Arayama Toru|title=Yagyū Onmyōken|year=2008|isbn=978-4-10-121042-1}} * {{ill|Azano Kōhei|ja|あざの耕平|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azano Kōhei|1=Kōhei Azano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11257270}}, ''Tokyo Ravens'' - A [[:en:Light novel|Light novel]] series featuring Onmyōji, including Kōtokui family members in a supporting role. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Chintaku Reifu Shrine|ja|鎮宅霊符神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chintaku Reifu Shrine|qq=}} * [[:en:Nara University of Education|Nara Educational University]] * {{ill|Kyobate Tenjinsha|ja|飛鳥神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyobate Tenjinsha|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|幸徳井家|version=100215448|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:幸徳井家]] {{wikidata link|Q11483987}} [[Category:Clans by region]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:History of Nara]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Kamo no Asomi Clan]] [[Category:Kuge]] [[Category:Kōtokuji Family]] [[Category:Onmyōdō]] [[Category:Onmyōryō]] [[Category:Soeage District]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
